treatments: started concurrent carboplatin auc 2 weekly with thoracic radiation for stage iiib unresectable nsclc.
initiated consolidation osimertinib 80 mg daily following completion of concurrent chemoradiation.
radiation: initiated concurrent thoracic radiation therapy to the right lower lobe primary and involved mediastinal lymph nodes, total dose 60 gy in 30 fractions over 6 weeks.
adverse_events: grade 2 esophagitis during concurrent chemoradiation, managed with viscous lidocaine and bland diet. weight loss of 4 kg.
grade 2 radiation dermatitis over the right posterior chest wall. managed with silver sulfadiazine cream.
grade 1 acneiform rash on the face, controlled with topical hydrocortisone.

treatments: at age 52, concurrent with radiation, patient was started on androgen deprivation therapy with leuprolide 45 mg intramuscular every 6 months, planned for 2 years.
at age 56, enzalutamide 160 mg orally daily was initiated for mcrpc; continued on leuprolide.
at age 58, olaparib 300 mg orally twice daily was initiated given pathogenic brca2 mutation; continued leuprolide.
at age 59, docetaxel 75 mg/m2 intravenously every 3 weeks with prednisone 5 mg twice daily was initiated for progressive mcrpc with visceral metastases.
radiation: at age 52, patient received definitive external beam radiation therapy (imrt) to the prostate and pelvic lymph nodes, total dose 79.2 gy to the prostate and 45 gy to the pelvis, in 44 fractions.
adverse_events: at age 56, patient developed grade 2 fatigue and grade 1 dizziness attributed to enzalutamide; dose was maintained with supportive care.
at age 58, patient developed grade 2 anemia (hemoglobin 9.2 g/dl) and grade 2 thrombocytopenia (platelets 68,000/ul) on olaparib; olaparib dose reduced to 250 mg twice daily.
at age 59, after cycle 2 of docetaxel, patient developed grade 3 neutropenia (anc 600/ul) requiring dose delay and pegfilgrastim support.

treatments: at age 66, patient started first-line folfox plus bevacizumab every 2 weeks with palliative intent.
at age 67, transitioned to second-line folfiri plus bevacizumab upon progression on folfox-based regimen.
at age 67, patient started tas-102 (trifluridine/tipiracil) as third-line therapy with continued bevacizumab.
adverse_events: after cycle 2 of folfox-bevacizumab, patient developed grade 1 proteinuria on routine urinalysis; monitored with urine protein-to-creatinine ratio.
at age 66, patient experienced grade 2 nausea and grade 1 mucositis on folfox, managed with ondansetron and magic mouthwash.

at age 67, patient developed grade 2 peripheral neuropathy (oxaliplatin-related) with difficulty buttoning shirts; oxaliplatin discontinued after 10 cycles, continued 5-fu/leucovorin plus bevacizumab maintenance.
at age 67, patient developed grade 2 diarrhea on folfiri with 5-6 loose stools daily, managed with loperamide.
at age 67, apixaban was held perioperatively when patient required percutaneous biliary drainage for biliary obstruction from hepatic metastases.

treatments: at age 67, combination ipilimumab 3 mg/kg plus nivolumab 1 mg/kg iv every 3 weeks was initiated for treatment of metastatic melanoma with brain metastases given high pd-l1 and high tmb.
at age 67, nivolumab 480 mg iv every 4 weeks was continued as maintenance immunotherapy after ipilimumab discontinuation.
at age 68, dabrafenib 150 mg twice daily plus trametinib 2 mg daily was initiated as second-line therapy for progressive braf v600e melanoma.
surgeries: at age 67, wide local excision with 2 cm margins was performed on the right scalp with rotation flap closure for local disease control despite known metastatic disease.
radiation: at age 67, stereotactic radiosurgery (srs) was delivered to the two brain metastases: 20 gy in 1 fraction to the right frontal lesion and 22 gy in 1 fraction to the left parietal lesion.
adverse_events: at age 67, after cycle 2 of ipilimumab/nivolumab, patient developed grade 3 immune-related colitis with bloody diarrhea (8-10 stools/day), requiring hospitalization, iv methylprednisolone 2 mg/kg, and discontinuation of ipilimumab.
at age 68, patient developed grade 2 fatigue and grade 1 arthralgias attributed to nivolumab, managed symptomatically without dose modification.

treatments: started concurrent carboplatin auc 2 weekly with thoracic radiation.
started consolidation osimertinib 80 mg daily after resolution of radiation pneumonitis and completion of steroid taper.
radiation: thoracic radiation therapy to left lower lobe primary with chest wall involvement and involved mediastinal lymph node stations, 60 gy in 30 fractions.
adverse_events: grade 2 radiation pneumonitis developing 4 weeks post-completion of radiation. treated with prednisone 40 mg with slow taper over 8 weeks.

mild recurrence of grade 1 pneumonitis-like symptoms 2 weeks after starting osimertinib. ct shows stable ground-glass opacities. managed with short course of prednisone 20 mg taper. osimertinib continued.

treatments: at age 40, started first-line tchp (docetaxel 75 mg/m2, carboplatin auc 6, trastuzumab, pertuzumab) every 21 days.
at age 41, completed 6 cycles tchp then transitioned to maintenance trastuzumab and pertuzumab every 21 days.
adverse_events: at age 40, baseline echocardiogram lvef 65%.
at age 41, after cycle 2 of tchp, developed grade 3 neutropenia requiring dose delay of 1 week. received pegfilgrastim prophylaxis for subsequent cycles.
at age 41, grade 2 nausea and grade 2 diarrhea on tchp. managed with ondansetron and loperamide.
at age 41, lvef 60% on surveillance echocardiogram. mild asymptomatic decline. continued therapy.
at age 41, grade 1 fatigue and intermittent grade 1 diarrhea on pertuzumab maintenance. tolerable.

treatments: at age 70, goserelin 10.8 mg subcutaneous every 3 months was initiated along with abiraterone acetate 1000 mg daily and prednisone 5 mg daily for de novo metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer.
at age 73, abiraterone was discontinued and docetaxel 75 mg/m2 intravenously every 3 weeks with prednisone 5 mg twice daily was initiated.
at age 74, cabazitaxel 20 mg/m2 intravenously every 3 weeks was initiated after disease progression on docetaxel.
radiation: at age 70, palliative radiation therapy 30 gy in 10 fractions was delivered to the lumbar spine (l2-l5) for severe pain and concern for cord compression.
adverse_events: at age 70, patient developed grade 2 hypertension (bp 168/98 mmhg) and grade 2 peripheral edema on abiraterone/prednisone; diuretic and antihypertensive regimen was adjusted.
at age 73, after cycle 2 of docetaxel, patient developed grade 3 neutropenia (anc 500/ul), grade 2 fatigue, and grade 2 peripheral neuropathy; dose reduced to 60 mg/m2 with pegfilgrastim support.
at age 74, patient reported grade 3 bone pain requiring hospitalization and initiation of patient-controlled analgesia with hydromorphone; palliative care was consulted.
at age 75, patient developed grade 3 diarrhea after cycle 1 of cabazitaxel requiring iv fluids and dose reduction to 15 mg/m2.

treatments: at age 73, cycle 1 of vrd completed with renal dose-adjusted lenalidomide; creatinine improved from 3.2 to 2.4 mg/dl; calcium normalized to 9.9 mg/dl; serum free lambda light chain decreased to 310 mg/l.

at age 73, cycles 3 and 4 of vrd completed with dose modifications; m-spike decreased to 1.8 g/dl; serum free lambda light chain 95 mg/l; creatinine improved to 1.8 mg/dl; classified as partial response per imwg criteria.
at age 74, started lenalidomide 5 mg days 1 through 21 with dexamethasone 20 mg weekly on 28-day cycles as maintenance; ongoing renal monitoring with creatinine stable at 1.7 mg/dl.

at age 74, started daratumumab-pomalidomide-dexamethasone (dpd) as second-line therapy; daratumumab 16 mg/kg weekly for 2 cycles then biweekly, pomalidomide 2 mg days 1 through 21 with renal dose adjustment, dexamethasone 20 mg weekly.
radiation: at age 73, received palliative radiation therapy to the sacral mass and t6 compression fracture site, 8 gy in a single fraction to each site for pain palliation.
adverse_events: at age 73, during cycle 2, patient developed grade 3 thrombocytopenia with platelets 38,000 and grade 2 neutropenia with anc 0.8; lenalidomide held for 10 days; platelet transfusion given; treatment resumed at lenalidomide 2.5 mg.

at age 73, after cycle 4, developed grade 2 peripheral neuropathy with bilateral foot pain and numbness; also grade 2 fatigue limiting daily activities; bortezomib reduced to 1.0 mg/m2 biweekly schedule.

at age 74, developed pneumocystis jirovecii pneumonia requiring hospitalization; treated with iv trimethoprim-sulfamethoxazole for 21 days; subsequently started on prophylactic trimethoprim-sulfamethoxazole after recovery.

treatments: at age 34, initiated neoadjuvant chemotherapy during second trimester: doxorubicin 60 mg/m2 and cyclophosphamide (ac) every 21 days for 4 cycles. her2-directed therapy deferred until after delivery.
at age 35, post-delivery, initiated taxane phase: paclitaxel 80 mg/m2 weekly for 12 weeks with trastuzumab and pertuzumab added.
at age 35, given pcr, continued adjuvant trastuzumab and pertuzumab to complete 1 year of her2-directed therapy.
surgeries: at age 35, underwent left modified radical mastectomy with axillary dissection. pathologic complete response (pcr) achieved: ypt0 ypn0. no residual invasive or in situ disease.
radiation: at age 35, received adjuvant chest wall and regional nodal radiation, 50 gy in 25 fractions.
adverse_events: at age 35, lvef 62% baseline, remained stable at 60% throughout treatment. tolerated well.
at age 34, during ac chemotherapy: grade 2 nausea, grade 2 alopecia, grade 1 fatigue. no febrile neutropenia episodes.

at age 35, during taxane/hp phase: grade 1 peripheral neuropathy, grade 1 diarrhea from pertuzumab. mild infusion-related reaction with trastuzumab first dose (grade 1 chills, resolved with slowing infusion).

treatments: at age 66, initiated carboplatin auc 5, pemetrexed 500 mg/m2, and pembrolizumab 200 mg iv every 3 weeks.
at age 66, completed 4 cycles of induction. transitioned to pemetrexed 500 mg/m2 plus pembrolizumab 200 mg iv every 3 weeks maintenance.
at age 67, resumed pemetrexed maintenance only after hepatitis resolution. pembrolizumab permanently discontinued given grade 2 hepatitis and subsequent concern for recurrence.
at age 67, initiated docetaxel 75 mg/m2 iv every 3 weeks as second-line therapy.
adverse_events: at age 66, after cycle 1, patient developed grade 1 nausea and grade 1 fatigue. well tolerated overall.
at age 66, after cycle 3, patient developed grade 2 fatigue and grade 1 constipation. creatinine mildly elevated at 1.2 mg/dl (baseline 1.0).
at age 67, during maintenance cycle 6, patient developed grade 2 immune-related hepatitis with alt 186 u/l and ast 142 u/l (both grade 2 elevation). pembrolizumab and pemetrexed held.
at age 67, hepatitis resolved over 4 weeks of corticosteroid therapy. alt and ast returned to normal. prednisone tapered over 6 weeks total.
at age 68, after cycle 3 of docetaxel, patient developed grade 2 fatigue and grade 2 peripheral neuropathy. docetaxel dose reduced to 60 mg/m2.

treatments: at age 49, combination ipilimumab 3 mg/kg plus nivolumab 1 mg/kg iv every 3 weeks was initiated for 4 induction cycles for metastatic braf v600e melanoma, with immunotherapy chosen first given high tmb.
at age 49, nivolumab 480 mg iv every 4 weeks was continued as maintenance after ipilimumab was permanently discontinued due to hepatotoxicity.
surgeries: at age 49, wide local excision with 2 cm margins of the left upper arm melanoma was performed with primary closure.
at age 49, sentinel lymph node biopsy of the left axilla revealed 2 of 3 sentinel nodes positive for metastatic melanoma with largest deposit measuring 4.8 mm.
adverse_events: at age 49, after cycle 3 of ipilimumab/nivolumab, patient developed grade 3 immune-related hepatitis with alt 425 u/l and ast 380 u/l, requiring ipilimumab discontinuation and high-dose iv methylprednisolone 2 mg/kg.
at age 50, patient developed grade 1 vitiligo affecting bilateral hands and periocular regions, considered a favorable prognostic sign and requiring no treatment intervention.

treatments: started osimertinib 80 mg daily for egfr l858r-mutant stage iv nsclc with pretreatment t790m.
surgeries: pericardiocentesis performed with drainage of 450 ml of serous pericardial fluid. cytology sent and confirmed positive for adenocarcinoma. pericardial drain placed and removed after 48 hours.
adverse_events: grade 1 stomatitis on osimertinib managed with gentle oral rinses. grade 1 decreased appetite.

treatments: at age 58, cycle 1 of vrd administered; patient tolerated treatment well with grade 1 insomnia and mild ankle edema attributed to dexamethasone.
at age 58, cycle 2 of vrd completed; repeat labs showed m-spike decreased to 2.0 g/dl; serum free lambda light chain decreased to 120 mg/l; hemoglobin stable at 10.5 g/dl.

at age 58, cycles 4 through 6 of vrd completed with dose modifications; post-induction spep showed m-spike 0.3 g/dl; serum free lambda light chain 28 mg/l; assessment classified as very good partial response per imwg criteria.

at age 59, patient received high-dose melphalan 200 mg/m2 conditioning followed by autologous stem cell transplant; stem cell dose infused was 4.8 million cd34-positive cells per kilogram; engraftment achieved on day plus 14.
at age 59, lenalidomide maintenance started at 10 mg days 1 through 21 of 28-day cycle; tolerated well with grade 1 fatigue; monthly cbc monitoring initiated.
adverse_events: at age 58, after cycle 3 of vrd, patient developed grade 2 thrombocytopenia with platelet count 62,000; lenalidomide dose reduced to 15 mg; also noted grade 1 peripheral neuropathy with intermittent fingertip tingling.

at age 59, post-transplant course complicated by grade 2 mucositis and grade 3 diarrhea requiring iv hydration; febrile neutropenia on day plus 7 treated with piperacillin-tazobactam; blood cultures negative; symptoms resolved by day plus 18.

at age 61, patient developed left lower extremity deep vein thrombosis while on lenalidomide maintenance; diagnosed by duplex ultrasound showing occlusive thrombus in the left popliteal vein; started on therapeutic enoxaparin then transitioned to apixaban; lenalidomide continued with close monitoring.

treatments: at age 63, initiated neoadjuvant carboplatin auc 5, paclitaxel 175 mg/m2, and pembrolizumab 200 mg iv every 3 weeks for 4 cycles.
at age 64, initiated adjuvant pembrolizumab 200 mg iv every 3 weeks for up to 1 year following surgery, per treatment protocol for resected nsclc after neoadjuvant chemoimmunotherapy.
surgeries: at age 64, patient underwent right upper lobectomy with mediastinal lymph node dissection. pathology showed major pathological response with only 5% viable tumor remaining. margins negative. lymph nodes 0/14 positive (complete nodal clearance). pathologic stage ypt1an0m0.
adverse_events: at age 63, after cycle 1, patient reported grade 1 nausea, grade 1 fatigue, and grade 1 alopecia.
at age 63, after cycle 3, patient developed grade 2 nausea requiring additional antiemetic therapy with olanzapine. grade 1 peripheral neuropathy noted.
at age 64, after cycle 4 of adjuvant pembrolizumab, patient developed grade 1 hypothyroidism with tsh 8.7 miu/l. initiated levothyroxine 50 mcg daily.

treatments: at age 50, initiated neoadjuvant folfirinox (5-fluorouracil 2400 mg/m2, leucovorin 400 mg/m2, irinotecan 180 mg/m2, oxaliplatin 85 mg/m2) every 14 days; cycle 1 of planned 6 cycles; full dosing given excellent performance status and young age.
at age 50, completed 6 cycles of neoadjuvant folfirinox; tolerated with manageable toxicity; grade 1 neuropathy in fingertips.
at age 51, began adjuvant gemcitabine 1000 mg/m2 days 1, 8, 15 every 28 days with capecitabine 1660 mg/m2/day days 1-21 every 28 days for planned 6 cycles.
at age 51, completed 6 cycles of adjuvant gemcitabine/capecitabine without dose modifications; ca 19-9 remained normal at 14 u/ml.
at age 51, started maintenance olaparib 300 mg twice daily; tolerated well with grade 1 nausea and grade 1 fatigue.
surgeries: at age 50, patient underwent laparoscopic distal pancreatectomy with splenectomy; intraoperative ultrasound confirmed mass in the pancreatic tail without gross vascular invasion; splenic artery and vein ligated; specimen sent for pathologic evaluation; estimated blood loss 200 ml; no complications.
adverse_events: at age 50, after cycle 2 of folfirinox, developed grade 2 nausea and grade 1 fatigue; managed with ondansetron and dexamethasone premedication.
at age 50, after cycle 4 of folfirinox, developed grade 2 neutropenia with anc 1.1 x 10^9/l and grade 1 diarrhea; pegfilgrastim added for subsequent cycles.
at age 51, during adjuvant therapy, developed grade 1 hand-foot syndrome and grade 1 fatigue; continued at full dose with emollient use.

treatments: at age 55, patient initiated cycle 1 of r-chop at standard doses with pegfilgrastim support starting day 2
at age 55, patient received cycle 2 of r-chop without complications, tolerating treatment well
at age 55, patient received cycle 3 of r-chop on schedule
at age 55, patient received cycles 4, 5, and 6 of r-chop, completing planned therapy without dose delays or modifications
adverse_events: at age 55, after cycle 2, patient developed grade 2 constipation and grade 1 peripheral neuropathy with mild paresthesias in feet, attributed to vincristine
at age 55, cumulative grade 2 neuropathy with numbness in bilateral feet persisted after completion of cycle 6, vincristine dose was reduced to 1 mg for cycles 5 and 6

treatments: at age 70, initiated first-line thp (docetaxel, trastuzumab, pertuzumab) with close cardiac monitoring given cardiac comorbidities. cardiology clearance obtained.
at age 71, docetaxel held after 4 cycles due to grade 2 peripheral neuropathy and cumulative fatigue. continued maintenance trastuzumab/pertuzumab plus letrozole.
at age 72, transitioned to second-line t-dm1 3.6 mg/kg every 21 days.
adverse_events: at age 70, baseline lvef 50% on echocardiogram. borderline low. cardio-oncology consultation obtained. continued her2-directed therapy with close surveillance.
at age 70, after cycle 1 of thp, developed grade 3 diarrhea requiring iv hydration. pertuzumab dose reduced for subsequent cycles.
at age 71, grade 2 nausea and grade 2 fatigue on thp. grade 1 peripheral edema. lvef monitored every 3 cycles.
at age 71, lvef 45% on echocardiogram. trastuzumab held. optimized heart failure medications (lisinopril uptitrated, carvedilol added). repeat echo in 4 weeks showed lvef 50%. resumed trastuzumab.
at age 72, grade 1 thrombocytopenia on t-dm1. lfts stable. cardiac function monitored closely given prior lvef decline.
at age 72, echocardiogram on t-dm1 showed lvef 48%. cardio-oncology recommends continuing with close monitoring. bnp mildly elevated at 180 pg/ml. no overt heart failure symptoms.

treatments: at age 58, initiated neoadjuvant folfirinox (5-fluorouracil 2400 mg/m2, leucovorin 400 mg/m2, irinotecan 180 mg/m2, oxaliplatin 85 mg/m2) every 14 days; cycle 1 of planned 8 cycles; prophylactic pegfilgrastim administered.
at age 58, completed 8 cycles of neoadjuvant folfirinox with dose modifications to oxaliplatin and irinotecan.
at age 59, began adjuvant gemcitabine 1000 mg/m2 days 1, 8, 15 every 28 days with capecitabine 1660 mg/m2/day days 1-21 every 28 days for planned 6 cycles.
at age 59, completed 6 cycles of adjuvant gemcitabine/capecitabine; ca 19-9 normal at 18 u/ml; transitioned to surveillance with ct every 3 months.
surgeries: at age 58, patient underwent classic pancreaticoduodenectomy (whipple procedure) with en bloc portal vein resection and primary venous anastomosis; intraoperative frozen sections of bile duct and pancreatic neck margins were negative; estimated blood loss 500 ml; operating time 6.5 hours.
adverse_events: at age 58, grade 3 biliary obstruction with total bilirubin peaking at 11.2 mg/dl with associated pruritus, dark urine, and acholic stools; resolved following ercp and biliary stent placement.
at age 58, after cycle 2 of folfirinox, patient developed grade 2 nausea and grade 2 diarrhea with 6 loose stools daily; managed with ondansetron, prochlorperazine, and loperamide.
at age 58, after cycle 5 of folfirinox, patient developed grade 2 thrombocytopenia with platelet count of 62 x 10^9/l; oxaliplatin dose reduced by 25% for subsequent cycles.
at age 59, during adjuvant chemotherapy cycle 4, patient developed grade 2 neutropenia and grade 1 fatigue; gemcitabine day 15 dose held; subsequent cycles continued with growth factor support.

treatments: at age 58, initiated carboplatin auc 5, pemetrexed 500 mg/m2, and pembrolizumab 200 mg iv every 3 weeks for metastatic nsclc.
at age 58, discontinued carboplatin/pemetrexed/pembrolizumab due to extracranial progression. initiated docetaxel 75 mg/m2 iv every 3 weeks as second-line therapy.
radiation: at age 58, received stereotactic radiosurgery (srs) to three brain metastases: right frontal 20 gy in 1 fraction, left parietal 20 gy in 1 fraction, right cerebellar 18 gy in 1 fraction.
adverse_events: at age 58, after cycle 2, patient developed grade 2 fatigue and grade 1 nausea. appetite decreased but maintaining oral intake.
at age 58, after cycle 3, patient developed grade 2 anemia (hemoglobin 8.9 g/dl). received one unit packed red blood cells with improvement.
at age 59, after cycle 2 of docetaxel, patient developed grade 2 fatigue and grade 1 mucositis. required dose reduction to 60 mg/m2.
at age 59, after cycle 6 of docetaxel, patient developed grade 2 peripheral neuropathy and persistent grade 2 fatigue impacting daily activities.

treatments: at age 56, received neoadjuvant was not given; proceeded with adjuvant tchp for 6 cycles followed by trastuzumab to complete 1 year.
at age 57, completed adjuvant trastuzumab. initiated adjuvant anastrozole 1 mg daily.
at age 58, initiated tucatinib 300 mg twice daily plus trastuzumab and capecitabine (tucatinib combination) given brain metastases and her2climb trial data.
surgeries: at age 56, underwent left partial mastectomy with sentinel lymph node biopsy. final pathology: 1.8 cm idc, 1 of 3 sentinel nodes with micrometastasis (0.8 mm), margins negative.
radiation: at age 56, adjuvant radiation therapy to the left breast and regional nodes, 42.56 gy in 16 fractions (hypofractionated) with a sequential boost of 10 gy in 4 fractions.
at age 58, underwent stereotactic radiosurgery (srs) to the 3 brain lesions: 20 gy single fraction to 2 smaller lesions, 18 gy single fraction to the 1.8 cm cerebellar lesion.
adverse_events: at age 56, during adjuvant tchp experienced grade 2 alopecia, grade 1 nausea, and grade 2 fatigue. lvef remained stable at 58%.
at age 58, developed grade 2 diarrhea on tucatinib/capecitabine, managed with dose reduction of capecitabine and loperamide.
at age 58, grade 2 hand-foot syndrome from capecitabine requiring topical emollients and brief treatment hold.

treatments: at age 50, initiated carboplatin auc 5, pemetrexed 500 mg/m2, and pembrolizumab 200 mg iv every 3 weeks. also started zoledronic acid 4 mg iv every 4 weeks for osseous metastasis.
at age 50, completed 4 cycles of induction. transitioned to pemetrexed 500 mg/m2 plus pembrolizumab 200 mg iv every 3 weeks maintenance.
adverse_events: at age 50, after cycle 1, patient developed grade 2 nausea despite prophylactic antiemetics. grade 1 fatigue. antiemetic regimen augmented with olanzapine.
at age 50, after cycle 2, patient developed grade 3 neutropenia (anc 0.8 x 10^9/l). received pegfilgrastim. carboplatin dose reduced to auc 4 for subsequent cycles.
at age 51, during maintenance cycle 3, patient developed grade 1 skin rash and grade 1 pruritus. managed with cetirizine and moisturizers.
at age 51, after maintenance cycle 10, patient developed grade 1 hypothyroidism (tsh 9.2 miu/l, free t4 low-normal). started levothyroxine 25 mcg daily.

treatments: at age 62, patient initiated cycle 1 of r-chop: rituximab 375 mg/m2, cyclophosphamide 750 mg/m2, doxorubicin 50 mg/m2, vincristine 1.4 mg/m2 capped at 2 mg, and prednisone 100 mg daily for 5 days
at age 62, patient received cycle 2 of r-chop with prophylactic pegfilgrastim, tolerated without significant complications
at age 62, patient received cycle 3 of r-chop on schedule with no dose modifications
at age 62, patient received cycle 4 of r-chop without dose modifications
at age 62, patient received cycle 5 of r-chop, vincristine dose maintained as neuropathy remained grade 1
at age 62, patient completed cycle 6 of r-chop without significant adverse events
adverse_events: at age 62, following cycle 1 of r-chop, patient developed grade 2 nausea and grade 1 fatigue managed with ondansetron and supportive care
at age 62, after cycle 4, patient reported grade 1 peripheral neuropathy with tingling in fingertips attributed to vincristine

treatments: at age 58, combination ipilimumab 3 mg/kg plus nivolumab 1 mg/kg iv every 3 weeks was initiated for 4 cycles for metastatic braf v600e melanoma with brain and hepatic involvement.
at age 58, after completing 4 cycles of ipilimumab/nivolumab induction, maintenance nivolumab 480 mg iv every 4 weeks was started.
surgeries: at age 58, wide local excision with 2 cm margins of the mid-back melanoma was performed with primary closure.
at age 58, sentinel lymph node biopsy of the bilateral axillae showed 2 of 5 total sentinel nodes positive for metastatic melanoma (1 right axillary, 1 left axillary), largest deposit 2.8 mm.
radiation: at age 58, stereotactic radiosurgery was delivered to the left temporal brain lesion at 24 gy in 1 fraction.
adverse_events: at age 58, patient developed grade 2 immune-related diarrhea after cycle 2 and grade 1 transaminase elevation (alt 95 u/l), managed with loperamide and close monitoring without treatment delay.

at age 58, blood glucose became more difficult to control with hemoglobin a1c rising from 6.8% to 8.2%, attributed to potential immune-mediated effect on pancreatic function and corticosteroid use; insulin was added to his diabetic regimen.

biomarkers: egfr c797s mutation detected in cis with l858r
treatments: started osimertinib 80 mg orally once daily as first-line treatment for egfr l858r-mutant stage iv nsclc.
discontinued osimertinib due to progressive disease with acquired c797s resistance. initiated carboplatin auc 5 plus pemetrexed 500 mg/m2 every 3 weeks.
adverse_events: grade 1 acneiform rash on the face and upper trunk, managed with topical clindamycin. no dose modification required.
grade 1 diarrhea, 3-4 loose stools daily, managed with loperamide as needed. tolerable and not requiring dose adjustment.
grade 2 nausea and fatigue following first cycle of carboplatin/pemetrexed. managed with ondansetron and dexamethasone.

treatments: at age 52, induction therapy was initiated with 7+3 cytarabine 200 mg/m2 continuous infusion and daunorubicin 60 mg/m2 combined with midostaurin 50 mg twice daily on days eight through twenty-one.

at age 52, given adverse risk features and mrd positivity, the patient was expedited to allogeneic stem cell transplant. he received one cycle of consolidation hidac 3 g/m2 plus midostaurin while a donor search was conducted.

at age 52, the patient underwent allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplant from an eight-of-eight hla-matched unrelated donor with myeloablative conditioning consisting of total body irradiation 1,200 cgy in six fractions and cyclophosphamide 60 mg/kg for two days.
adverse_events: at age 52, the patient developed neutropenic fever on day six with absolute neutrophil count of 0. ct chest showed a peripheral nodule with surrounding halo sign in the left upper lobe concerning for angioinvasive aspergillosis. serum galactomannan was positive at 2.8 index and voriconazole was initiated.
at age 52, hyperglycemia was difficult to control during induction requiring insulin drip and later transition to a basal-bolus insulin regimen with total daily dose of 86 units.

at age 52, the patient developed sinusoidal obstruction syndrome (hepatic veno-occlusive disease) on day plus 12 with painful hepatomegaly, weight gain of six kilograms, and bilirubin rising to 8.4 mg/dl, treated with defibrotide.
at age 52, grade 1 acute gvhd of the skin developed with maculopapular rash over less than 25% body surface area, managed with topical corticosteroids alone.

treatments: at age 48, given double-hit biology, consideration given to dose-adjusted r-epoch versus r-chop; after discussion, patient initiated cycle 1 of r-chop at standard doses with plan for close interim assessment
at age 48, patient received cycle 2 of r-chop with pegfilgrastim, no significant toxicity
at age 48, patient received cycle 3 of r-chop on schedule
at age 48, encouraged by interim response, patient completed cycles 4, 5, and 6 of r-chop without dose modifications or significant toxicity
adverse_events: at age 48, after cycle 1 of r-chop, patient experienced grade 1 nausea and grade 1 fatigue only

treatments: at age 55, received adjuvant tchp (docetaxel, carboplatin, trastuzumab, pertuzumab) for 6 cycles followed by maintenance trastuzumab to complete 1 year.
at age 56, completed adjuvant trastuzumab. started adjuvant letrozole 2.5 mg daily planned for 10 years.
at age 60, initiated first-line for metastatic disease: thp regimen (docetaxel, trastuzumab, pertuzumab) every 21 days given recurrence more than 12 months after adjuvant trastuzumab.
at age 61, discontinued docetaxel after 6 cycles and continued maintenance trastuzumab/pertuzumab. resumed letrozole.
at age 62, transitioned to second-line t-dm1 3.6 mg/kg every 21 days.
surgeries: at age 55, underwent left breast lumpectomy with sentinel lymph node biopsy. pathology confirmed clear margins and 0/2 nodes positive.
radiation: at age 55, received adjuvant whole breast radiation therapy, 50 gy in 25 fractions with a 10 gy boost to the tumor bed.
adverse_events: at age 55, during adjuvant tchp developed grade 2 nausea, grade 2 alopecia, and grade 1 fatigue. managed supportively.
at age 60, baseline lvef 60% on echocardiogram.
at age 61, echocardiogram showed lvef 54%. asymptomatic. continued therapy with close monitoring.
at age 61, developed grade 2 peripheral neuropathy from cumulative docetaxel. persisted after discontinuation. started duloxetine 60 mg daily.

treatments: started osimertinib 80 mg daily for egfr exon 19 deletion-positive stage iv nsclc with brain metastases.
radiation: srs to left occipital brain metastasis (14 mm) at 21 gy in single fraction and right parietal brain metastasis (9 mm) at 21 gy in single fraction.
adverse_events: grade 1 diarrhea with 2-3 loose stools per day on osimertinib. self-limited and not requiring treatment.

treatments: at age 45, patient initiated cycle 1 of r-chop with tumor lysis syndrome prophylaxis including rasburicase, allopurinol, and aggressive hydration given high tumor burden
at age 45, patient received cycle 2 of r-chop with dose reduction of cyclophosphamide by 25 percent due to prior neutropenic fever, with pegfilgrastim support
at age 45, patient received cycle 3 of r-chop with continued dose-reduced cyclophosphamide
at age 45, patient received cycles 4, 5, and 6 of r-chop with increasing neuropathy symptoms
at age 45, patient initiated salvage therapy with r-ice: rituximab 375 mg/m2, ifosfamide 5000 mg/m2, carboplatin auc 5, etoposide 100 mg/m2 days 1-3
at age 45, patient completed 3 cycles of r-ice salvage therapy
at age 46, patient received beam conditioning (carmustine, etoposide, cytarabine, melphalan) followed by autologous hematopoietic stem cell transplant
surgeries: at age 46, patient underwent successful autologous stem cell collection with adequate cd34+ cell yield of 5.2 million cells/kg
adverse_events: at age 45, during cycle 1 of r-chop, patient developed grade 3 neutropenic fever requiring hospitalization and iv broad-spectrum antibiotics with piperacillin-tazobactam for 5 days
at age 45, after cycle 5, patient developed grade 2 peripheral neuropathy with numbness and pain in hands and feet, vincristine reduced by 50 percent for cycle 6
at age 45, after cycle 1 of r-ice, patient developed grade 4 neutropenia and grade 3 thrombocytopenia requiring platelet transfusion and growth factor support
at age 46, post-transplant course complicated by grade 3 mucositis and febrile neutropenia requiring iv antibiotics for 10 days

treatments: at age 57, leuprolide 22.5 mg intramuscular every 3 months was initiated concurrent with salvage radiation, planned for 6 months.
at age 60, leuprolide 45 mg intramuscular every 6 months was restarted for second biochemical recurrence; intermittent adt was not offered given high-risk features.
at age 61, enzalutamide 160 mg orally daily was initiated for mcrpc; leuprolide continued.
at age 63, enzalutamide was discontinued and olaparib 300 mg orally twice daily was initiated given brca1 pathogenic mutation; leuprolide continued.
surgeries: at age 55, patient underwent robot-assisted radical prostatectomy with extended pelvic lymph node dissection; pathologic stage pt3bn0m0 with seminal vesicle invasion and negative surgical margins.
radiation: at age 57, salvage radiation therapy was delivered to the prostate bed and pelvic lymph nodes, total dose 70 gy and 50.4 gy respectively, with concurrent short-term adt.
adverse_events: at age 63, patient developed grade 1 nausea and grade 2 fatigue on olaparib; managed with ondansetron and dose maintained.
at age 63, patient reported grade 2 bone pain in the lumbar spine requiring scheduled oxycodone 10 mg every 8 hours.

treatments: initiated osimertinib 80 mg orally daily for egfr exon 19 deletion-positive stage iv nsclc.
started zoledronic acid 4 mg iv every 4 weeks for bone metastases.
radiation: stereotactic radiosurgery (srs) to right frontal lobe metastasis (8 mm) delivering 21 gy in single fraction, and left cerebellar metastasis (6 mm) delivering 21 gy in single fraction. treatment tolerated well with dexamethasone taper.
adverse_events: grade 1 dry skin and paronychia of bilateral great toes, managed with moisturizers and warm soaks. no dose modification.

treatments: at age 71, cycle 1 of vrd completed; creatinine improved to 2.1 mg/dl with hydration; serum free kappa light chain decreased to 420 mg/l; calcium normalized to 9.8 mg/dl with zoledronic acid and hydration.

at age 71, cycles 3 and 4 of vrd completed with dose modifications; serum free kappa light chain decreased to 85 mg/l; creatinine improved to 1.5 mg/dl; hemoglobin improved to 9.8 g/dl with erythropoiesis-stimulating agent support.
at age 72, transitioned to lenalidomide maintenance at 5 mg daily days 1 through 21 of 28-day cycle with ongoing monthly monitoring; zoledronic acid continued every 3 months.

at age 73, initiated dkd regimen with daratumumab 16 mg/kg weekly for 2 cycles then biweekly, carfilzomib 56 mg/m2 on days 1, 8, and 15, and dexamethasone 20 mg twice weekly on treatment days; echocardiogram prior to carfilzomib showed ef 55%.
radiation: at age 71, patient received urgent palliative radiation therapy to the l1 vertebral body and paraspinal mass, 20 gy delivered in 5 fractions for pain control and prevention of further structural compromise; concurrent initiation of iv zoledronic acid with dose adjustment for renal impairment.
adverse_events: at age 71, after cycle 2, patient developed grade 3 neutropenia with anc 0.6 and grade 2 thrombocytopenia with platelets 55,000; lenalidomide held for 1 week then resumed at 5 mg; g-csf support added.
at age 71, after cycle 4, patient developed grade 2 peripheral neuropathy with painful dysesthesias in bilateral feet and hands; bortezomib reduced to 1.0 mg/m2 and schedule changed to biweekly.
at age 73, after cycle 2 of dkd, patient developed grade 2 hypertension attributed to carfilzomib requiring addition of amlodipine 5 mg daily; blood pressure subsequently controlled.

treatments: started erlotinib 150 mg daily for egfr l858r-mutant stage iv nsclc.
started zoledronic acid 4 mg iv every 4 weeks for bone metastases.
discontinued erlotinib. started cisplatin 80 mg/m2 day 1 and etoposide 100 mg/m2 days 1-3, every 3 weeks for small cell transformation.
adverse_events: grade 2 acneiform rash on face, scalp, and upper trunk. grade 2 diarrhea with 5-6 stools daily, requiring loperamide and dose reduction consideration.
erlotinib dose reduced from 150 mg to 100 mg daily due to persistent grade 2 diarrhea not adequately controlled with loperamide.
grade 3 neutropenia after first cycle of cisplatin/etoposide. managed with pegfilgrastim. grade 2 nausea and vomiting.

treatments: at age 61, induction therapy was initiated with 7+3 cytarabine and daunorubicin plus midostaurin per the ratify protocol dosing schedule.

at age 61, flag-ida salvage chemotherapy was administered with fludarabine 30 mg/m2 on days one through five, cytarabine 2 g/m2 on days one through five, g-csf starting day zero, and idarubicin 8 mg/m2 on days three through five.
at age 61, given persistent disease and flt3-itd positivity, gilteritinib 120 mg orally daily was initiated as targeted salvage therapy.
adverse_events: at age 61, during induction the patient developed disseminated intravascular coagulation with fibrinogen dropping to 62 mg/dl, d-dimer rising to 28 ug/ml, and prolonged pt to 22.4 seconds. she required cryoprecipitate and platelet transfusions with gradual improvement.
at age 61, neutropenic fever occurred on day eight with blood cultures growing enterococcus faecium resistant to vancomycin, treated with daptomycin.

at age 61, the patient developed invasive pulmonary mucormycosis during aplasia following flag-ida, with ct showing a reverse halo sign in the right lower lobe. isavuconazonium was initiated and she required a prolonged hospitalization of 42 days.
at age 61, gilteritinib caused differentiation syndrome with fever, dyspnea, bilateral pleural effusions, and weight gain of eight pounds, managed with dexamethasone and temporary drug hold.

treatments: at age 44, received neoadjuvant tchp for 6 cycles.
at age 45, given pcr, continued adjuvant trastuzumab and pertuzumab to complete 1 year of her2-directed therapy (per aphinity approach).
at age 46, initiated trastuzumab deruxtecan (t-dxd) 5.4 mg/kg every 21 days given brain metastasis and recent prior trastuzumab exposure.
surgeries: at age 45, underwent left total mastectomy with sentinel lymph node biopsy. pathologic complete response (pcr): ypt0 ypn0. no residual invasive or in situ carcinoma. 0 of 4 sentinel nodes positive.
radiation: at age 45, adjuvant chest wall radiation 50 gy in 25 fractions given initial node-positive disease (cn1).
at age 46, underwent stereotactic radiosurgery to the left parietal brain metastasis, 21 gy single fraction.
adverse_events: at age 44, baseline lvef 64%. during tchp: grade 2 alopecia, grade 2 nausea, grade 1 fatigue, grade 2 neutropenia managed with pegfilgrastim.
at age 45, lvef 60% at mid-adjuvant therapy. stable. no cardiac symptoms.
at age 46, on t-dxd: grade 2 nausea managed with antiemetics, grade 1 fatigue, grade 1 decreased appetite. baseline ct chest clear for ild monitoring.

treatments: at age 55, cycles 1 through 4 of vrd completed without significant toxicity; grade 1 fatigue and intermittent grade 1 constipation only adverse effects noted.

at age 55, completed 2 additional cycles of vrd for total of 6 cycles; post-induction spep showed no detectable m-spike; immunofixation with faint igg kappa band; serum free light chain ratio normalized at 1.3; deepening to near-complete response.

at age 56, patient underwent stem cell mobilization with plerixafor plus g-csf; collected 8.1 million cd34-positive cells per kilogram; proceeded to high-dose melphalan 200 mg/m2 conditioning followed by autologous stem cell transplant.
at age 56, started lenalidomide maintenance at 10 mg days 1 through 21 of 28-day cycle; excellent tolerability with no dose modifications required.
adverse_events: at age 56, post-transplant course uncomplicated with grade 1 mucositis only; engraftment on day plus 11; no febrile neutropenia; discharged on day plus 14.
at age 60, developed grade 1 skin rash on trunk attributed to lenalidomide; managed with topical corticosteroids; lenalidomide continued without dose change.

treatments: at age 68, the patient was started on venetoclax 400 mg daily (with a ramp-up over four days) in combination with azacitidine 75 mg/m2 subcutaneously for seven days on a 28-day cycle, with midostaurin 50 mg twice daily added on days eight through twenty-one given flt3-itd positivity.
at age 68, venetoclax-azacitidine was continued with dose reduction of venetoclax to 14 days per cycle due to prolonged cytopenias. midostaurin was continued on schedule.
adverse_events: at age 68, during cycle one the patient developed tumor lysis syndrome with potassium 5.8 meq/l and uric acid 10.1 mg/dl during venetoclax ramp-up, managed with intravenous hydration and rasburicase.

at age 68, prolonged cytopenias following cycle one resulted in neutropenic fever with temperature 38.6 degrees celsius on day 16. blood cultures grew coagulase-negative staphylococcus from the central line and the patient was treated with vancomycin and the line was removed.
at age 68, during cycle four the patient was hospitalized for candida glabrata fungemia attributed to prolonged neutropenia and central venous catheter, treated with micafungin and catheter removal.
at age 68, the patient developed progressive renal insufficiency with creatinine rising to 2.1 mg/dl attributed to repeated contrast exposure and tumor lysis, requiring venetoclax dose adjustment.

treatments: at age 57, patient initiated pembrolizumab 200 mg iv every 3 weeks as first-line therapy for msi-high metastatic colorectal cancer.
at age 58, patient continued pembrolizumab maintenance with excellent tolerance and no evidence of active disease on imaging.
surgeries: at age 58, patient underwent laparoscopic right hemicolectomy for the primary cecal tumor given excellent response to immunotherapy.
adverse_events: after 3 cycles of pembrolizumab, patient developed grade 1 pruritic maculopapular rash on trunk, managed with topical corticosteroids.
at age 58, patient developed grade 2 immune-related hypothyroidism with tsh of 32 miu/l, started on levothyroxine 75 mcg daily.
at age 58, patient experienced grade 1 fatigue attributed to pembrolizumab and managed hypothyroidism, not requiring treatment modification.

treatments: started osimertinib 80 mg daily for egfr exon 19 deletion-positive stage iv nsclc.
started denosumab 120 mg subcutaneously every 4 weeks for bone metastatic disease.
adverse_events: grade 1 skin dryness and periungual fissures. managed with moisturizer and cotton gloves at night.

treatments: at age 52, adjuvant nivolumab 480 mg iv every 4 weeks was initiated for resected stage iiic melanoma per checkmate 238 protocol.
at age 54, combination dabrafenib 150 mg twice daily plus trametinib 2 mg daily was initiated for braf v600e-mutant stage iv melanoma given rapid disease progression.
surgeries: at age 52, wide local excision with 2 cm margins of the primary melanoma site on the upper back was performed with primary closure.
at age 52, sentinel lymph node biopsy of the right axilla was performed, with 2 of 4 sentinel nodes positive for metastatic melanoma deposits measuring up to 3.2 mm.
at age 52, completion right axillary lymph node dissection was performed, yielding 18 lymph nodes with no additional nodes positive for metastatic disease (0/14 additional nodes).
adverse_events: at age 53, patient developed grade 2 immune-related diarrhea with 5-6 loose stools daily, managed with loperamide and close monitoring without treatment interruption.

at age 54, patient developed grade 2 pyrexia (temperature 39.1 c) attributed to dabrafenib, managed with dose interruption and acetaminophen, with rechallenge at reduced dabrafenib dose of 100 mg twice daily.

treatments: at age 58, leuprolide 45 mg intramuscular every 6 months was initiated along with abiraterone 1000 mg daily and prednisone 5 mg daily for metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer.
at age 62, abiraterone was discontinued and olaparib 300 mg orally twice daily was initiated given brca2 pathogenic mutation detected on ctdna; continued leuprolide.
at age 65, docetaxel 60 mg/m2 intravenously every 3 weeks (dose-reduced for renal impairment) with prednisone 5 mg twice daily was initiated.
radiation: at age 64, palliative radiation therapy 20 gy in 5 fractions was delivered to the t6 vertebral body for progressive pain despite opioids.
adverse_events: at age 58, patient developed grade 2 hyperglycemia (hba1c rose from 7.2% to 9.1%) on prednisone requiring insulin initiation; also grade 1 hypokalemia managed with supplementation.

at age 62, patient developed grade 2 anemia (hemoglobin 8.9 g/dl) and grade 1 elevated creatinine (from 1.6 to 1.9 mg/dl) on olaparib; transfusion of 2 units packed red blood cells was given and olaparib dose was reduced to 250 mg twice daily.
at age 64, patient developed grade 2 bone pain in the thoracic spine requiring scheduled long-acting morphine 30 mg every 12 hours.

at age 65, after cycle 2 of docetaxel, patient developed grade 3 febrile neutropenia (anc 300/ul, temperature 39.1c) requiring hospitalization, iv antibiotics, and pegfilgrastim; docetaxel dose further reduced to 50 mg/m2.

treatments: at age 55, adjuvant dabrafenib 150 mg twice daily plus trametinib 2 mg daily was initiated for resected stage iiid melanoma.
at age 57, combination ipilimumab 3 mg/kg plus nivolumab 1 mg/kg iv every 3 weeks was initiated for metastatic melanoma given prior exposure to braf/mek inhibitor therapy in the adjuvant setting.
surgeries: at age 55, wide local excision with 2 cm margins of the right thigh melanoma was performed with primary closure and all margins were confirmed negative.
at age 55, sentinel lymph node biopsy of the right inguinal region showed 3 of 4 sentinel nodes positive for metastatic melanoma, largest deposit measuring 12 mm with extracapsular extension.
at age 55, completion right inguinal and iliac lymph node dissection was performed yielding 16 nodes, of which 4 additional nodes were positive (total 7 of 20 nodes positive).
adverse_events: at age 55, patient developed grade 3 pyrexia (temperature 40.1 c) with rigors and hypotension within 10 days of starting dabrafenib/trametinib, requiring emergency department visit, iv fluids, and dabrafenib dose reduction to 75 mg twice daily.
at age 55, despite dose reduction, patient experienced recurrent grade 2 pyrexia episodes, and dabrafenib was further managed with prophylactic corticosteroid protocol with improved tolerability.
at age 57, after cycle 2 of ipilimumab/nivolumab, patient developed grade 2 immune-related diarrhea (5-6 stools/day) managed with oral budesonide and loperamide without treatment interruption.

treatments: at age 71, patient began folfirinox (5-fluorouracil 2400 mg/m2 46-hour infusion, leucovorin 400 mg/m2, irinotecan 150 mg/m2, oxaliplatin 85 mg/m2) every 14 days; dose-attenuated with irinotecan reduced to 135 mg/m2 given age and cardiac history; cycle 1 of planned 12 cycles.
at age 71, completed 10 cycles of folfirinox/modified folfiri; transitioned to chemoradiation.
at age 72, patient began maintenance olaparib 300 mg twice daily as part of clinical trial protocol following completion of first-line platinum-based chemotherapy and chemoradiation.
radiation: at age 71, patient received concurrent chemoradiation with capecitabine 825 mg/m2 twice daily on radiation days; 50.4 gy in 28 fractions delivered to the pancreatic body mass and regional lymph nodes using intensity-modulated radiation therapy.
adverse_events: at age 71, after cycle 3 of folfirinox, patient developed grade 2 nausea and grade 2 fatigue; required iv hydration and addition of olanzapine to antiemetic regimen.

at age 71, after cycle 6 of folfirinox, patient developed grade 2 peripheral neuropathy with numbness and tingling in fingers and toes affecting fine motor tasks; oxaliplatin discontinued from regimen.
at age 71, during chemoradiation, patient experienced grade 2 nausea, grade 1 diarrhea, and grade 2 fatigue; managed with supportive care; completed all 28 fractions.
at age 72, patient developed grade 2 anemia on olaparib with hemoglobin of 9.2 g/dl; managed with dose reduction of olaparib to 250 mg twice daily.

treatments: at age 66, leuprolide 22.5 mg intramuscular every 3 months was initiated concurrent with radiation, planned for 2 years given high-risk features.
at age 70, leuprolide 22.5 mg intramuscular every 3 months was restarted for biochemical recurrence.
at age 71, abiraterone acetate 1000 mg daily with prednisone 5 mg twice daily was added to continued leuprolide for mcrpc.
at age 73, docetaxel 75 mg/m2 intravenously every 3 weeks with prednisone 5 mg twice daily was initiated.
radiation: at age 66, patient received external beam radiation therapy (vmat) to the prostate, total dose 78 gy in 39 fractions, with concurrent adt.
at age 74, palliative radiation therapy 8 gy in a single fraction was delivered to the right proximal humerus for severe pain limiting arm function.
adverse_events: at age 71, patient developed grade 2 hyperglycemia (fasting glucose 246 mg/dl) on abiraterone/prednisone requiring adjustment of diabetic medications; also grade 1 peripheral edema.
at age 73, after cycle 1 of docetaxel, patient developed grade 2 fatigue and grade 1 peripheral neuropathy; also noted worsening glycemic control.
at age 74, patient sustained a compression fracture of t11 vertebral body; was started on denosumab 120 mg subcutaneously every 4 weeks for skeletal-related event prevention.

treatments: at age 28, induction chemotherapy was initiated with 7+3 cytarabine 200 mg/m2 continuous infusion for seven days and daunorubicin 60 mg/m2 on days one through three, with midostaurin 50 mg orally twice daily on days eight through twenty-one.

at age 28, consolidation consisted of four cycles of high-dose cytarabine 3 g/m2 every twelve hours on days one, three, and five, with midostaurin 50 mg twice daily on days eight through twenty-one per cycle.
at age 28, midostaurin maintenance 50 mg orally twice daily was initiated after consolidation, planned for twelve cycles.
adverse_events: at age 28, the patient developed neutropenic fever on day eleven with temperature 39.8 degrees celsius. ct chest showed tree-in-bud nodularity and ground-glass opacities. bronchoalveolar lavage grew aspergillus fumigatus and voriconazole was started with midostaurin dose held temporarily due to drug interaction.
at age 28, grade 3 mucositis developed during the aplastic phase requiring iv narcotics and tpn for nutritional support lasting twelve days.
at age 28, during consolidation cycle two the patient developed febrile neutropenia with klebsiella pneumoniae bacteremia requiring a 14-day course of meropenem.
at age 28, midostaurin maintenance was complicated by persistent grade 2 nausea and grade 1 diarrhea requiring dose timing adjustment to be taken with food and addition of prophylactic ondansetron.

treatments: started osimertinib 80 mg daily for egfr l858r-mutant stage iv nsclc with brain metastases.
started zoledronic acid 4 mg iv every 4 weeks for bone metastases.
radiation: stereotactic radiosurgery to three brain metastases: right frontal 18 gy, left temporal 20 gy, and right cerebellar 20 gy, all in single fractions.
adverse_events: grade 1 fatigue and headache in the week following srs. dexamethasone taper over 2 weeks.
grade 1 skin rash on extremities. grade 1 paronychia. both manageable without intervention.

treatments: started osimertinib 80 mg daily after completion of wbrt for egfr exon 19 deletion-positive nsclc with brain metastases.
started denosumab 120 mg subcutaneously every 4 weeks for bone metastases.
osimertinib dose escalated from 80 mg to 160 mg daily for leptomeningeal carcinomatosis to increase cns drug levels.
radiation: whole brain radiation therapy (wbrt) delivering 30 gy in 10 fractions with hippocampal avoidance technique plus memantine to reduce neurocognitive effects.
adverse_events: grade 2 fatigue during and following wbrt. grade 1 alopecia. short-term memory difficulties reported.
grade 1 rash and grade 1 diarrhea on osimertinib. self-limited.

treatments: at age 53, initiated first-line thp (docetaxel, trastuzumab, pertuzumab) every 21 days plus zoledronic acid for bone metastasis.
at age 54, docetaxel discontinued after 6 cycles. continued maintenance trastuzumab/pertuzumab plus letrozole 2.5 mg daily (postmenopausal).
at age 55, transitioned to second-line t-dm1 3.6 mg/kg every 21 days.
adverse_events: at age 53, baseline lvef 62% on muga scan.
at age 53, after cycle 3 of thp: grade 2 nausea, grade 2 alopecia, grade 1 myalgia, and grade 2 diarrhea from pertuzumab.
at age 54, lvef 57% on surveillance echocardiogram. asymptomatic. continued treatment.
at age 55, on t-dm1: grade 1 fatigue, grade 1 elevated ast (42), platelets stable at 140,000. tolerable side effect profile.

treatments: at age 47, initiated first-line thp regimen: docetaxel 75 mg/m2, trastuzumab 8 mg/kg loading then 6 mg/kg, and pertuzumab 840 mg loading then 420 mg, every 21 days.
at age 48, after 6 cycles of thp, docetaxel discontinued due to cumulative neuropathy grade 2. continued maintenance trastuzumab and pertuzumab every 21 days.
at age 49, continued maintenance trastuzumab and pertuzumab. added letrozole 2.5 mg daily for er-positive disease given postmenopausal status.
at age 50, transitioned to second-line t-dm1 (ado-trastuzumab emtansine) 3.6 mg/kg every 21 days due to disease progression.
adverse_events: at age 47, baseline echocardiogram showed lvef 62%, within normal limits prior to initiation of her2-directed therapy.
at age 48, after cycle 3 of thp, patient developed grade 2 nausea managed with ondansetron. grade 2 alopecia noted. mild peripheral neuropathy grade 1 in fingertips.
at age 48, repeat echocardiogram after 4 cycles showed lvef 58%, slight decline from baseline but within acceptable range. continued her2-directed therapy.
at age 48, developed grade 1 diarrhea attributed to pertuzumab, managed with loperamide as needed.
at age 49, echocardiogram showed lvef 55%, monitored closely. no symptoms of heart failure.
at age 50, after 2 cycles of t-dm1, developed grade 2 thrombocytopenia (platelets 68,000). dose reduced to 3.0 mg/kg.

treatments: at age 58, received neoadjuvant tchp (docetaxel, carboplatin, trastuzumab, pertuzumab) for 6 cycles.
at age 59, given residual disease, received adjuvant t-dm1 for 14 cycles per katherine protocol.
at age 60, initiated trastuzumab deruxtecan (t-dxd, enhertu) 5.4 mg/kg every 21 days given cns activity data and brain metastases.
surgeries: at age 58, underwent right modified radical mastectomy with axillary lymph node dissection. residual disease present after neoadjuvant therapy.
radiation: at age 59, adjuvant radiation to the right chest wall and regional nodes, 50 gy in 25 fractions with 10 gy boost to mastectomy scar.
at age 60, underwent whole brain radiation therapy (wbrt) 30 gy in 10 fractions with hippocampal avoidance technique, plus srs boost to the 2 largest lesions (18 gy single fraction each).
adverse_events: at age 58, during neoadjuvant tchp: grade 3 neutropenia (received pegfilgrastim), grade 2 nausea, grade 2 alopecia, grade 2 diarrhea. lvef 58% stable.
at age 59, during adjuvant t-dm1: grade 2 thrombocytopenia (nadir 65,000, dose reduced to 3.0 mg/kg), grade 1 fatigue, grade 1 elevated ast. lvef 55%.
at age 60, on t-dxd: grade 2 nausea (managed with ondansetron and dexamethasone), grade 1 fatigue, grade 1 alopecia. monitored for interstitial lung disease with regular ct assessment.

treatments: at age 42, received adjuvant paclitaxel weekly for 12 weeks with trastuzumab (th regimen per apt trial) followed by trastuzumab to complete 1 year.
at age 43, completed adjuvant trastuzumab. initiated adjuvant tamoxifen 20 mg daily (premenopausal).
at age 44, initiated first-line for metastatic disease: thp (docetaxel, trastuzumab, pertuzumab) every 21 days, given recurrence more than 12 months after adjuvant trastuzumab.
at age 44, after 6 cycles of thp, switched to maintenance hp with fulvestrant 500 mg (given esr1 mutation rendering tamoxifen and aromatase inhibitors less effective).
surgeries: at age 42, underwent left breast lumpectomy with sentinel lymph node biopsy. final pathology: 1.3 cm idc, margins negative (closest margin 4 mm), 0 of 3 sentinel lymph nodes positive.
radiation: at age 42, adjuvant whole breast radiation, 40 gy in 15 fractions (hypofractionated).
adverse_events: at age 42, during weekly paclitaxel/trastuzumab: grade 1 alopecia, grade 1 fatigue, no significant neuropathy. lvef stable at 63%.
at age 44, baseline lvef 60%. after cycle 4 of thp, lvef 56%. grade 2 nausea and grade 2 peripheral neuropathy developed.

treatments: at age 58, induction chemotherapy was initiated with the 7+3 regimen of cytarabine 200 mg/m2 continuous infusion for seven days and daunorubicin 60 mg/m2 on days one through three, plus midostaurin 50 mg orally twice daily on days eight through twenty-one.

at age 58, the patient received one cycle of consolidation with high-dose cytarabine 1.5 g/m2 (age-adjusted dosing) every twelve hours on days one, three, and five, plus midostaurin on days eight through twenty-one.

at age 58, given adverse eln risk and mrd positivity, the patient was referred for allogeneic stem cell transplant and underwent haploidentical transplant from her son with post-transplant cyclophosphamide-based gvhd prophylaxis and reduced-intensity conditioning with fludarabine and melphalan.
at age 59, the patient was started on gilteritinib 120 mg orally daily as flt3-directed salvage therapy for relapsed aml post-transplant.
adverse_events: at age 58, the patient developed neutropenic fever on day seven with temperature of 38.9 degrees celsius and absolute neutrophil count of 80/ul. blood cultures were negative but ct sinuses showed acute invasive fungal sinusitis, and liposomal amphotericin b was started empirically.
at age 58, grade 2 nausea and vomiting occurred during induction requiring ondansetron and olanzapine for antiemetic control.

at age 58, during consolidation the patient experienced grade 3 cerebellar toxicity manifesting as ataxia and dysmetria, attributed to high-dose cytarabine, which resolved over seven days after cytarabine was held.

at age 58, the patient developed grade 3 acute gvhd of the lower gastrointestinal tract with profuse watery diarrhea exceeding 1,500 ml per day, requiring high-dose methylprednisolone and ruxolitinib as second-line therapy.
at age 59, gilteritinib was complicated by qtc prolongation to 508 ms requiring dose reduction to 80 mg daily and close cardiac monitoring.

treatments: at age 67, initiated pembrolizumab 200 mg iv every 3 weeks as first-line monotherapy for metastatic nsclc with high pd-l1 expression.
at age 67, continued pembrolizumab 200 mg iv every 3 weeks, cycles 5 through 12.
at age 68, continued pembrolizumab maintenance, cycles 13 through 24.
at age 69, continued pembrolizumab 200 mg iv every 3 weeks.
adverse_events: at age 67, after cycle 2 of pembrolizumab, patient reported grade 1 fatigue and mild arthralgias managed with acetaminophen.
at age 68, after cycle 8 of pembrolizumab, patient developed grade 2 hypothyroidism with tsh 14.2 miu/l. started levothyroxine 75 mcg daily.
at age 68, patient reported persistent grade 1 fatigue and grade 1 pruritus. managed symptomatically.

treatments: at age 68, cycle 1 of vrd completed; spep at end of cycle 1 showed m-spike decreased to 3.8 g/dl indicating early response.
at age 68, cycle 2 of vrd completed; m-spike further decreased to 2.2 g/dl; serum free kappa light chain decreased to 125 mg/l; hemoglobin improved to 10.1 g/dl.

at age 68, cycles 4 through 6 of vrd completed with dose-reduced bortezomib; post-induction spep showed m-spike 0.5 g/dl; serum free kappa light chain 28 mg/l; response classified as very good partial response.
at age 69, received high-dose melphalan 200 mg/m2 followed by autologous stem cell transplant; cell dose 4.2 million cd34-positive cells per kilogram.
at age 69, started lenalidomide maintenance at 10 mg with plan for indefinite treatment given high-risk cytogenetics with t(4;14) and gain(1q21).
radiation: at age 68, patient received prophylactic radiation therapy to the left femoral neck lesion, 20 gy in 5 fractions, to reduce fracture risk; concurrent initiation of denosumab 120 mg subcutaneously monthly given creatinine clearance of 52 ml/min.
adverse_events: at age 68, after cycle 3, patient developed grade 2 peripheral neuropathy in bilateral lower extremities and grade 2 constipation; bortezomib dose reduced to 1.0 mg/m2; aggressive bowel regimen instituted.

at age 69, post-transplant complicated by grade 3 mucositis, grade 2 diarrhea, and febrile neutropenia on day plus 8; blood cultures grew coagulase-negative staphylococcus treated with vancomycin; engraftment on day plus 15.
at age 70, developed grade 2 fatigue and grade 1 anemia with hemoglobin 10.8 g/dl on lenalidomide maintenance; dose reduced to 5 mg; fatigue improved.

treatments: at age 52, cycles 1 through 4 of vrd completed; tolerated exceptionally well with only grade 1 insomnia related to dexamethasone; no neuropathy, no cytopenias; m-spike decreased from 3.5 to 0.6 g/dl after cycle 4.

at age 52, completed cycles 5 and 6 of vrd; post-induction spep showed no detectable m-spike; immunofixation with very faint igg kappa band; serum free light chain ratio 1.4; deepened to near-complete response.

at age 53, proceeded to stem cell mobilization with g-csf alone; collected 10.2 million cd34-positive cells per kilogram given young age and excellent marrow reserve; sufficient for potential tandem transplant if needed in the future.

at age 53, received high-dose melphalan 200 mg/m2 conditioning followed by autologous stem cell transplant with infusion of 5.0 million cd34-positive cells per kilogram; remaining cells cryopreserved for potential future use.
at age 53, started lenalidomide maintenance at 10 mg days 1 through 21 of 28-day cycle; tolerated without difficulty.
adverse_events: at age 53, post-transplant course notable for grade 2 mucositis and grade 1 nausea; no infectious complications; engraftment on day plus 10; rapid count recovery; discharged day plus 12.
at age 56, developed grade 2 neutropenia with anc 0.7 during a viral upper respiratory infection; lenalidomide held for 2 weeks then resumed at 10 mg without recurrence of neutropenia.

treatments: started erlotinib 150 mg daily for egfr l858r-mutant stage iv nsclc. chemotherapy avoided due to significant cardiopulmonary comorbidities.

after goals of care discussion, continued erlotinib with best supportive care. patient not a candidate for further systemic therapy given comorbidities. enrolled in home hospice with continued erlotinib for symptom management.
surgeries: tunneled pleural catheter (pleurx) placed for management of symptomatic left malignant pleural effusion.
adverse_events: grade 1 rash on face. grade 1 diarrhea. erlotinib tolerated without cardiac exacerbation at 2-week follow-up.
grade 2 anorexia and weight loss of 3 kg. nutritional counseling initiated. likely multifactorial (disease, erlotinib, and comorbidities).

treatments: at age 54, initiated carboplatin auc 5, pemetrexed 500 mg/m2, and pembrolizumab 200 mg iv every 3 weeks. also started denosumab 120 mg subcutaneous every 4 weeks for osseous metastatic disease.
at age 54, completed 4 cycles of induction. transitioned to pemetrexed 500 mg/m2 plus pembrolizumab 200 mg iv every 3 weeks maintenance.
at age 55, initiated docetaxel 75 mg/m2 plus ramucirumab 10 mg/kg iv every 3 weeks as second-line therapy.
radiation: at age 54, received palliative radiation therapy to right 7th rib pathologic fracture site, 30 gy in 10 fractions.
at age 54, received stereotactic radiosurgery to two left occipital lobe brain metastases, 20 gy in 1 fraction each.
adverse_events: at age 54, after cycle 1, patient developed grade 2 nausea and grade 1 fatigue. antiemetic regimen adjusted with addition of olanzapine.
at age 54, after cycle 3, patient developed grade 2 fatigue and grade 1 anemia (hemoglobin 10.2 g/dl).
at age 55, after cycle 2 of docetaxel/ramucirumab, patient developed grade 2 fatigue, grade 1 peripheral neuropathy, and grade 1 epistaxis. no hypertension noted.

treatments: at age 47, adjuvant androgen deprivation therapy with leuprolide 22.5 mg intramuscular every 3 months was initiated postoperatively given node-positive disease, planned for 24 months.
at age 51, leuprolide 45 mg intramuscular every 6 months was restarted for biochemical recurrence; continuous adt was planned given prior node-positive disease.
at age 53, enzalutamide 160 mg orally daily was added to leuprolide for mcrpc.
at age 55, olaparib 300 mg orally twice daily was initiated given confirmed brca2 pathogenic deletion; continued leuprolide.
at age 56, olaparib was discontinued and docetaxel 75 mg/m2 intravenously every 3 weeks with prednisone 5 mg twice daily was initiated for progressive mcrpc with visceral (pulmonary) metastases.
surgeries: at age 47, patient underwent robot-assisted radical prostatectomy with extended pelvic lymph node dissection; pathologic stage pt3bn1m0 with seminal vesicle invasion and 2 of 24 lymph nodes positive for metastatic carcinoma.
radiation: at age 48, adjuvant radiation therapy was delivered to the prostate bed and pelvic lymph nodes, total dose 66 gy and 45 gy respectively, while on adt.
adverse_events: at age 54, patient developed grade 2 fatigue significantly impacting daily activities and grade 1 gynecomastia while on enzalutamide and leuprolide.
at age 55, patient developed grade 2 anemia (hemoglobin 9.4 g/dl), grade 1 nausea, and grade 1 dysgeusia on olaparib; iron supplementation and antiemetics were provided.

at age 56, after cycle 3 of docetaxel, patient developed grade 3 neutropenia (anc 700/ul) and grade 2 peripheral neuropathy in the feet; pegfilgrastim was added and docetaxel dose reduced to 60 mg/m2.

treatments: started erlotinib 150 mg orally once daily for egfr exon 19 deletion-positive stage iv nsclc.
initiated denosumab 120 mg subcutaneously every 4 weeks for bone metastatic disease.
discontinued erlotinib. initiated osimertinib 80 mg daily for t790m-positive egfr-mutant nsclc after progression on first-generation tki.
adverse_events: grade 2 acneiform rash on face, chest, and back within 2 weeks of starting erlotinib. managed with doxycycline 100 mg bid and topical clindamycin. grade 2 diarrhea requiring loperamide.
grade 1 dry skin and mild paronychia after switching to osimertinib. rash significantly improved compared to erlotinib.

treatments: at age 60, patient was admitted for initiation of cycle 1 of r-chop with aggressive tumor lysis syndrome prophylaxis including rasburicase, allopurinol, iv fluids at 200 ml/hr, and cardiac monitoring
at age 60, patient received cycle 2 of r-chop with 25 percent dose reduction of all agents and mandatory pegfilgrastim, prophylactic levofloxacin and fluconazole added
at age 60, patient received cycle 3 of r-chop at reduced doses
at age 60, patient switched to salvage r-ice: rituximab 375 mg/m2, ifosfamide 5000 mg/m2 with mesna, carboplatin auc 5, etoposide 100 mg/m2 days 1 through 3
at age 60, patient received cycle 2 of r-ice with dose reduction, complicated by prolonged cytopenias
at age 61, patient completed cycle 3 of r-ice salvage and underwent successful autologous stem cell collection yielding 4.8 million cd34+ cells/kg
surgeries: at age 61, patient received beam conditioning chemotherapy followed by autologous hematopoietic stem cell transplant
adverse_events: at age 60, during cycle 1, patient developed laboratory tumor lysis syndrome with potassium 5.8, phosphorus 6.9, uric acid 12.4, and ldh spike to 1,240 u/l, managed with rasburicase re-dosing, aggressive hydration, and electrolyte monitoring in the icu

at age 60, on day 8 of cycle 1, patient developed grade 4 febrile neutropenia with anc less than 100, temperature 104.1f, and hypotension requiring vasopressor support, treated with broad-spectrum antibiotics including meropenem and vancomycin
at age 60, after cycle 1 of r-ice, patient developed grade 3 mucositis, grade 4 neutropenia, and grade 3 thrombocytopenia with platelet nadir 12,000 requiring multiple platelet transfusions
at age 61, post-transplant course complicated by grade 4 mucositis, clostridioides difficile colitis treated with oral vancomycin, and prolonged neutropenia with engraftment on day 16

treatments: at age 65, started first-line thp (docetaxel, trastuzumab, pertuzumab) with denosumab 120 mg monthly for bone metastases.
at age 66, continued thp through 6 cycles. docetaxel discontinued; maintenance trastuzumab/pertuzumab continued.
at age 67, transitioned to second-line t-dm1 3.6 mg/kg every 21 days.
adverse_events: at age 65, baseline lvef 55% on echocardiogram. noted mild diastolic dysfunction attributed to hypertension.
at age 66, after cycle 2 of thp developed grade 2 nausea, grade 2 alopecia, and grade 1 peripheral neuropathy. grade 1 elevated ast.
at age 66, lvef declined to 48% on surveillance echocardiogram. trastuzumab held for 1 cycle. started lisinopril and carvedilol. repeat echo showed recovery to 52%. resumed her2-directed therapy.
at age 67, after 3 cycles of t-dm1, developed grade 2 thrombocytopenia (platelets 72,000) and grade 1 elevated transaminases. dose reduced to 3.0 mg/kg.
at age 67, grade 2 fatigue and grade 1 nausea on t-dm1. peripheral neuropathy stable at grade 1.

treatments: at age 59, leuprolide 22.5 mg intramuscular every 3 months was initiated for second biochemical recurrence.
at age 59, enzalutamide 160 mg orally daily was added to leuprolide for mcrpc.
at age 61, docetaxel 75 mg/m2 intravenously every 3 weeks with prednisone 5 mg twice daily was initiated following progression on enzalutamide.
surgeries: at age 53, patient underwent robot-assisted radical prostatectomy with bilateral pelvic lymph node dissection; pathologic stage pt2cn0m0 with negative surgical margins and 0 of 14 lymph nodes positive.
radiation: at age 56, salvage radiation therapy was delivered to the prostate bed, total dose 66 gy in 33 fractions.
adverse_events: at age 60, patient developed grade 2 fatigue and grade 1 cognitive impairment (difficulty with word-finding) attributed to enzalutamide; discussed with patient who elected to continue therapy.
at age 61, after cycle 3 of docetaxel, patient developed grade 2 neutropenia (anc 1100/ul), grade 2 mucositis, and grade 1 alopecia; treatment continued with supportive care.

treatments: at age 74, after geriatric oncology assessment, patient initiated cycle 1 of r-minichop (dose-attenuated r-chop per lysa protocol for elderly): rituximab 375 mg/m2, cyclophosphamide 400 mg/m2, doxorubicin 25 mg/m2, vincristine 1 mg flat dose, prednisone 40 mg/m2
at age 74, patient received cycle 2 of r-minichop, tolerated reasonably well
at age 74, patient received cycle 3 of r-minichop with prophylactic levofloxacin added
at age 74, patient received cycles 4, 5, and 6 of r-minichop, completing planned treatment
adverse_events: at age 74, after cycle 1, patient developed grade 2 fatigue and grade 1 nausea, with grade 2 neutropenia managed with pegfilgrastim
at age 74, after cycle 2, patient was hospitalized for grade 3 neutropenic fever with anc 320 and temperature 102.8f, treated with ceftriaxone and discharged after 4 days
at age 74, after cycle 4, patient developed grade 2 peripheral neuropathy with difficulty walking, vincristine discontinued for cycles 5 and 6
at age 74, after cycle 5, patient developed herpes zoster on right t4 dermatome, treated with valacyclovir and continued on prophylaxis

treatments: at age 66, patient was initiated on androgen deprivation therapy with goserelin 10.8 mg subcutaneous implant every 3 months plus docetaxel 75 mg/m2 every 3 weeks for 6 cycles for metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer.
at age 69, abiraterone acetate 1000 mg orally once daily with prednisone 5 mg twice daily was added to continued goserelin for mcrpc.
at age 70, cabazitaxel 25 mg/m2 intravenously every 3 weeks with prednisone 5 mg daily was initiated as next-line chemotherapy given prior docetaxel exposure.
radiation: at age 71, palliative radiation therapy 20 gy in 5 fractions was delivered to the t8 vertebral body for intractable pain and concern for epidural extension.
adverse_events: at age 66, during cycle 4 of docetaxel, patient developed grade 2 neutropenia (anc 1200/ul) and grade 2 fatigue; growth factor support with pegfilgrastim was initiated.
at age 69, patient developed grade 2 hypertension (bp 162/96 mmhg) and grade 1 hypokalemia (k 3.2 meq/l) attributed to abiraterone; lisinopril 10 mg and potassium supplementation were started.
at age 71, patient developed grade 3 neutropenic fever (anc 400/ul, temperature 38.9c) after cycle 2 of cabazitaxel requiring hospitalization and iv antibiotics; cabazitaxel dose reduced to 20 mg/m2.
at age 71, patient reported grade 3 bone pain in the thoracic spine requiring conversion from oral oxycodone to fentanyl patch 50 mcg/hour.

treatments: at age 67, initiated folfirinox (5-fluorouracil 2400 mg/m2, leucovorin 400 mg/m2, irinotecan 150 mg/m2, oxaliplatin 85 mg/m2) every 14 days; cycle 1 of planned 12 cycles; port-a-cath placed for venous access.
at age 67, continued modified folfirinox through cycle 10 with ongoing dose reductions; cumulative grade 2 peripheral neuropathy developed; oxaliplatin discontinued after cycle 8.
at age 68, initiated second-line gemcitabine 1000 mg/m2 with nab-paclitaxel 125 mg/m2 on days 1, 8, 15 of 28-day cycles; palliative intent.
radiation: at age 68, following completion of 10 cycles of chemotherapy, patient received sbrt (stereotactic body radiation therapy) to the pancreatic body/tail mass, 33 gy in 5 fractions over 10 days with fiducial marker-guided treatment; concurrent low-dose capecitabine as radiosensitizer.
adverse_events: at age 67, after cycle 1 of folfirinox, patient developed grade 3 nausea and grade 2 vomiting despite three-drug antiemetic prophylaxis; required 48-hour hospital admission for iv hydration and antiemetic management.

at age 67, after cycle 4 of folfirinox, patient developed grade 3 neutropenia with anc 0.6 x 10^9/l and grade 2 diarrhea; subsequent cycles dose-reduced with irinotecan decreased by 25% and pegfilgrastim added.
at age 68, patient developed grade 2 peripheral neuropathy with significant numbness in feet affecting balance and daily activities; oxaliplatin permanently discontinued.
at age 68, after cycle 2 of gemcitabine/nab-paclitaxel, patient developed grade 2 fatigue, grade 1 alopecia, and worsening grade 2 neuropathy; nab-paclitaxel dose reduced to 100 mg/m2.

treatments: at age 76, initiated pembrolizumab 200 mg iv every 3 weeks as first-line monotherapy for metastatic squamous nsclc with high pd-l1 expression.
at age 76, continued pembrolizumab 200 mg iv every 3 weeks.
at age 77, resumed pembrolizumab 200 mg iv every 3 weeks after resolution of colitis.
adverse_events: at age 76, after cycle 1 of pembrolizumab, patient developed grade 2 fatigue requiring increased rest periods. grade 1 decreased appetite continued.
at age 76, after cycle 3 of pembrolizumab, patient developed grade 1 diarrhea (3 loose stools daily) and grade 1 pruritic rash on upper extremities.
at age 77, after cycle 10, patient developed grade 2 colitis with 5-6 loose stools daily and mild abdominal cramping. pembrolizumab held. started budesonide 9 mg daily.

treatments: at age 48, patient initiated first-line folfox (oxaliplatin 85 mg/m2, 5-fu, leucovorin) plus bevacizumab 5 mg/kg every 2 weeks with goal of liver metastasis downsizing for resection.
at age 49, postoperative folfox (without bevacizumab) resumed for 4 additional cycles to complete perioperative chemotherapy.
surgeries: at age 49, patient underwent laparoscopic wedge resection of segment vi hepatic metastasis with intraoperative ultrasound confirmation of no additional lesions.
at age 49, patient underwent low anterior resection of rectosigmoid tumor with total mesorectal excision and diverting loop ileostomy.
at age 49, loop ileostomy was reversed after confirmation of anastomotic integrity by contrast enema.
radiation: at age 49, postoperative short-course radiation therapy (25 gy in 5 fractions) was delivered to the pelvis given close circumferential margin concerns, though final margin was negative.
adverse_events: after cycle 3 of folfox, patient developed grade 1 peripheral neuropathy with tingling in fingertips, monitored without dose change.
at age 49, patient developed grade 2 peripheral neuropathy after cumulative 10 cycles of oxaliplatin, with numbness in hands and feet affecting fine motor tasks.

treatments: at age 62, leuprolide 22.5 mg intramuscular every 3 months was initiated with salvage radiation, planned for 24 months given high-risk recurrence features.
at age 66, leuprolide 45 mg intramuscular every 6 months was restarted for oligometastatic recurrence.
at age 68, enzalutamide 160 mg orally daily was added to continued leuprolide for mcrpc.
at age 70, enzalutamide was discontinued and docetaxel 75 mg/m2 intravenously every 3 weeks with prednisone 5 mg twice daily was started.
surgeries: at age 60, patient underwent robot-assisted radical prostatectomy with bilateral pelvic lymph node dissection; pathologic stage pt3an0m0 with focal positive margin at the right apex.
radiation: at age 62, salvage radiation therapy was delivered to the prostate bed, total dose 68 gy in 34 fractions, with concurrent adt.
at age 66, stereotactic body radiation therapy (sbrt) 35 gy in 5 fractions was delivered to the right iliac bone metastasis for metastasis-directed therapy.
adverse_events: at age 69, patient developed grade 2 hypertension (bp 156/94 mmhg) on enzalutamide requiring addition of hydrochlorothiazide to his regimen.
at age 70, after cycle 4 of docetaxel, patient developed grade 2 peripheral neuropathy with numbness in hands and feet, grade 2 fatigue, and grade 1 nail changes; dose reduced to 60 mg/m2.

treatments: at age 72, combination dabrafenib 150 mg twice daily plus trametinib 2 mg daily was initiated for braf v600e-mutant stage iv melanoma with brain metastasis given need for rapid disease control.
at age 73, dabrafenib/trametinib was discontinued and nivolumab 480 mg iv every 4 weeks was initiated as second-line therapy.
surgeries: at age 72, wide local excision with 2 cm margins of the anterior chest melanoma was performed for local disease control with advancement flap closure.
at age 72, sentinel lymph node biopsy of the left axilla showed 1 of 2 sentinel nodes positive for macrometastatic melanoma deposit measuring 8.2 mm.
radiation: at age 72, stereotactic radiosurgery was delivered to the right cerebellar metastasis at a dose of 21 gy in 1 fraction.
adverse_events: at age 72, patient developed grade 2 pyrexia (temperature 39.5 c) and grade 2 fatigue within the first week of dabrafenib/trametinib, managed with dose interruption of dabrafenib for 3 days and restart at 100 mg twice daily.
at age 72, patient developed grade 2 diarrhea (4-6 stools/day above baseline) attributed to trametinib, managed with loperamide.
