treatments: at age 62, patient began neoadjuvant folfirinox (5-fluorouracil 2400 mg/m2 46-hour infusion, leucovorin 400 mg/m2, irinotecan 150 mg/m2, oxaliplatin 85 mg/m2) every 14 days, cycle 1 of planned 8 cycles.
at age 62, patient completed 8 cycles of neoadjuvant folfirinox with dose modifications; cumulative grade 1 peripheral neuropathy in hands and feet noted.
at age 63, patient began adjuvant gemcitabine 1000 mg/m2 on days 1, 8, 15 of 28-day cycles combined with capecitabine 1660 mg/m2/day on days 1-21 of 28-day cycles, for planned 6 cycles.
at age 63, patient completed 6 cycles of adjuvant gemcitabine/capecitabine with dose modifications; transitioned to surveillance.
surgeries: at age 62, patient underwent a pancreaticoduodenectomy (whipple procedure) with portal vein resection and reconstruction; operative findings included a firm mass in the pancreatic head with adherence to the portal vein requiring segmental resection; estimated blood loss 650 ml; no intraoperative complications.
adverse_events: at age 62, patient experienced grade 2 biliary obstruction with total bilirubin of 8.4 mg/dl and direct bilirubin of 6.1 mg/dl, which resolved to near-normal levels following ercp with stent placement.
at age 62, after cycle 2 of folfirinox, patient developed grade 3 neutropenia with anc of 0.8 x 10^9/l; cycle 3 was delayed by one week and irinotecan dose was reduced by 20%.
at age 62, after cycle 4 of folfirinox, patient reported grade 2 diarrhea with 5-6 loose stools per day and grade 1 nausea managed with ondansetron and loperamide.
at age 63, patient developed grade 2 pancreatic exocrine insufficiency post-whipple requiring increased pancreatic enzyme supplementation; weight stabilized after dietary optimization.
at age 63, during cycle 3 of adjuvant gemcitabine/capecitabine, patient developed grade 2 hand-foot syndrome with painful erythema and desquamation of palms; capecitabine dose reduced by 25%.

treatments: started erlotinib 150 mg orally once daily for egfr l858r-mutant stage iv nsclc.
discontinued erlotinib. started osimertinib 80 mg daily for t790m-positive progressive disease.
radiation: stereotactic radiosurgery to left parietal brain metastasis, 20 gy in single fraction. short course of dexamethasone 4 mg bid with taper.
adverse_events: grade 2 rash on face and trunk. grade 1 diarrhea. grade 1 elevated creatinine (from baseline 1.4 to 1.7 mg/dl). monitored renal function closely.
grade 1 qtc prolongation on osimertinib (qtc 475 ms from baseline 440 ms). cardiology consulted given history of atrial fibrillation. continued with ecg monitoring.

treatments: at age 74, initiated gemcitabine 1000 mg/m2 with nab-paclitaxel 125 mg/m2 on days 1, 8, 15 of 28-day cycles; cycle 1 of planned indefinite treatment pending response and tolerability.
at age 75, continued gemcitabine/nab-paclitaxel with dose modifications through cycle 8; ca 19-9 further decreased to 487 u/ml.
at age 75, resumed maintenance gemcitabine monotherapy 1000 mg/m2 days 1, 8, 15 of 28-day cycles; nab-paclitaxel discontinued due to cumulative neuropathy.
radiation: at age 75, following 8 cycles of gemcitabine/nab-paclitaxel, patient received consolidative sbrt to pancreatic head mass, 33 gy in 5 fractions; treatment well tolerated with grade 1 nausea only.
adverse_events: at age 74, grade 3 biliary obstruction with total bilirubin 14.6 mg/dl, associated coagulopathy with inr 1.8, and malaise; apixaban held prior to ercp; resolved following stent placement and vitamin k supplementation.
at age 74, after cycle 1 of gemcitabine/nab-paclitaxel, developed grade 2 thrombocytopenia with platelet count 58 x 10^9/l and grade 2 fatigue; day 15 nab-paclitaxel held.
at age 74, after cycle 3, developed grade 2 peripheral neuropathy and grade 2 neutropenia; nab-paclitaxel dose reduced to 100 mg/m2 for subsequent cycles.

at age 75, patient developed grade 3 weight loss with current weight 104 lbs (down from 118 lbs at diagnosis); increased pancreatic enzyme dosing and oral nutritional supplementation; megestrol acetate added for appetite stimulation.

treatments: at age 59, patient started first-line folfiri (irinotecan 180 mg/m2, 5-fu, leucovorin) plus bevacizumab 5 mg/kg every 2 weeks, chosen over folfox due to pre-existing mild neuropathy from bilateral carpal tunnel syndrome.

at age 60, resumed folfiri-bevacizumab postoperatively; liver surgery deferred due to multifocal residual disease not amenable to safe resection; planned for continued systemic therapy with reevaluation.
surgeries: at age 60, given sustained response with low-volume residual hepatic disease, patient underwent laparoscopic right hemicolectomy to address the primary tumor.
adverse_events: after cycle 2 of folfiri-bevacizumab, patient developed grade 2 diarrhea and grade 2 nausea; managed with loperamide and dose reduction of irinotecan to 150 mg/m2.
at age 59, patient experienced grade 2 hypertension (bp 158/94) on bevacizumab, requiring increase of existing lisinopril and addition of hydrochlorothiazide.
at age 60, patient developed grade 1 proteinuria (urine protein-to-creatinine ratio 0.8) on bevacizumab; monitored without dose change.
at age 60, patient experienced grade 2 fatigue limiting daily activities; discussed treatment holiday versus continued therapy; patient elected to continue with supportive care.

treatments: started osimertinib 80 mg daily for egfr exon 19 deletion-positive stage iv nsclc.
started denosumab 120 mg subcutaneously every 4 weeks for bone metastases.
radiation: palliative radiation to t9 vertebral body metastasis, 20 gy in 5 fractions for pain control and stabilization.
adverse_events: grade 1 dry skin and mild paronychia on osimertinib.

grade 1 interstitial lung disease suspected at 22 months. ct shows new bilateral ground-glass opacities in lower lobes not present on prior imaging, distinct from tumor. pulmonary function tests show mild restrictive pattern. osimertinib held.

treatments: at age 62, cycle 1 of vrd (bortezomib, lenalidomide, dexamethasone) induction was administered; patient tolerated treatment with grade 1 nausea and mild constipation.

at age 62, cycles 3 and 4 of vrd completed with dose-modified bortezomib; repeat spep showed m-spike decreased to 1.1 g/dl; serum free kappa light chain decreased to 42 mg/l; assessment consistent with very good partial response per imwg criteria.

at age 62, patient underwent stem cell mobilization with cyclophosphamide 2 g/m2 followed by g-csf 10 mcg/kg daily; adequate cd34-positive stem cells collected at 5.2 million cells per kilogram over 2 days of apheresis.

at age 62, patient received high-dose melphalan conditioning at 200 mg/m2 followed by autologous stem cell transplant; engraftment achieved on day plus 12 with anc greater than 500 and platelet count greater than 20,000 by day plus 16.
at age 63, patient started lenalidomide maintenance therapy at 10 mg orally days 1 through 21 of a 28-day cycle; cbc monitored monthly with dose adjustments planned for cytopenias.

at age 66, patient started daratumumab-lenalidomide-dexamethasone (drd) salvage regimen with daratumumab 16 mg/kg iv weekly for cycles 1 and 2, then biweekly, lenalidomide 25 mg days 1 through 21, and dexamethasone 40 mg weekly on a 28-day cycle.
radiation: at age 66, patient received palliative external beam radiation therapy to the right femoral shaft lesion, 30 gy delivered in 10 fractions over 2 weeks for pain control and fracture prevention.
adverse_events: at age 62, after cycle 2 of vrd, patient developed grade 2 peripheral neuropathy with numbness and tingling in bilateral feet; bortezomib dose reduced to 1.0 mg/m2 and changed to weekly dosing schedule.

at age 62, during post-transplant recovery, patient developed grade 3 mucositis requiring iv narcotics and total parenteral nutrition for 5 days; also had febrile neutropenia managed with broad-spectrum antibiotics.
at age 63, after 4 months of lenalidomide maintenance, patient developed grade 2 neutropenia with anc 0.9; lenalidomide held for 1 week then resumed at 5 mg daily.

at age 65, patient developed herpes zoster reactivation on the left t10 dermatome despite acyclovir prophylaxis; treated with valacyclovir 1 g three times daily for 7 days with resolution; acyclovir prophylaxis dose increased.

at age 66, during first daratumumab infusion, patient experienced grade 2 infusion-related reaction with chills, nasal congestion, and mild throat tightness; managed with iv diphenhydramine and acetaminophen; subsequent infusions tolerated with premedication.

treatments: started osimertinib 80 mg daily for egfr exon 19 deletion-positive stage iv nsclc.
surgeries: pleurx catheter placed for management of symptomatic right malignant pleural effusion. draining approximately 500 ml every other day.
pleurx catheter removed after successful trial of clamping with no significant fluid reaccumulation. pleurodesis achieved spontaneously.
adverse_events: grade 1 mucositis and dry mouth on osimertinib. managed with oral care and hydration.

treatments: at age 45, patient started first-line pembrolizumab 200 mg iv every 3 weeks for msi-high metastatic colorectal cancer.
at age 46, resumed pembrolizumab 4 weeks postoperatively; plan to complete 2 years total duration.
surgeries: at age 46, patient underwent elective laparoscopic low anterior resection of rectosigmoid primary to address ongoing intermittent bleeding and tenesmus from residual primary tumor.
adverse_events: at age 46, patient developed grade 2 immune-related colitis with 4-6 watery stools daily and cramping; managed with oral budesonide 9 mg daily without need for pembrolizumab interruption.
at age 46, patient experienced grade 1 arthralgia in bilateral knees and wrists attributed to immune-related adverse effect; managed with naproxen.

treatments: at age 51, received neoadjuvant tchp (docetaxel, carboplatin, trastuzumab, pertuzumab) for 6 cycles.
at age 51, given residual disease after neoadjuvant therapy, received adjuvant t-dm1 for 14 cycles per katherine trial protocol.
at age 52, initiated tamoxifen 20 mg daily given er-positive disease and male sex.
surgeries: at age 51, underwent right modified radical mastectomy with level i/ii axillary lymph node dissection. residual disease present.
radiation: at age 52, received adjuvant chest wall and regional nodal radiation, 50 gy in 25 fractions.
adverse_events: at age 51, during neoadjuvant tchp: grade 2 alopecia, grade 2 nausea, grade 1 thrombocytopenia. lvef stable at 60%.
at age 52, on adjuvant t-dm1 experienced grade 1 fatigue and grade 1 peripheral neuropathy. platelets remained above 100,000.
at age 52, lvef 58% on completion echocardiogram. within normal limits. annual cardiac surveillance recommended.

treatments: at age 63, patient initiated folfox plus bevacizumab every 2 weeks as first-line therapy.
at age 64, resumed postoperative folfox for 4 additional cycles (12 total perioperative); bevacizumab held for 8 weeks postoperatively per surgical protocol.
surgeries: at age 64, patient underwent laparoscopic sigmoid colectomy, segment vi wedge resection, and segment iii wedge resection in a single operative session.
adverse_events: after cycle 4 of folfox, patient developed grade 2 cold-triggered peripheral neuropathy in hands with moderate interference with daily activities; also noted grade 1 thrombocytopenia.

at age 64, cumulative oxaliplatin neuropathy progressed to grade 2 with persistent numbness in hands and feet and difficulty with fine motor tasks; completed planned 12 cycles but with last 2 at 75% oxaliplatin dose.
at age 64, patient developed grade 1 hepatic steatosis noted on surveillance imaging, attributed to combination of chemotherapy and diabetes-related fatty liver.

treatments: at age 71, patient initiated cycle 1 of r-chop with reduced doxorubicin dose at 40 mg/m2 due to age and mild renal impairment, with aggressive antiemetic regimen and tumor lysis prophylaxis
at age 71, patient received cycle 2 of r-chop with rituximab administered at reduced rate, tolerated without infusion reaction
at age 71, patient received cycle 3 of r-chop with further dose reduction of cyclophosphamide by 25 percent and mandatory pegfilgrastim support
at age 71, patient received cycles 4, 5, and 6 of modified r-cvp without doxorubicin
adverse_events: at age 71, during cycle 1 of r-chop patient experienced grade 2 infusion-related reaction to rituximab with rigors and hypotension requiring slowing of infusion rate and premedication adjustment
at age 71, after cycle 2, patient developed grade 3 neutropenic fever with blood cultures positive for escherichia coli bacteremia requiring hospitalization and iv meropenem for 14 days
at age 71, after cycle 3, echocardiogram showed ejection fraction decreased to 45 percent from baseline of 58 percent, concerning for doxorubicin-related cardiotoxicity
at age 71, during cycle 5, patient developed grade 2 peripheral neuropathy with difficulty buttoning clothes, vincristine dose reduced by 50 percent

treatments: at age 54, initiated first-line folfox plus bevacizumab every 2 weeks.
at age 55, postoperative folfox resumed for 4 cycles to complete perioperative chemotherapy plan; bevacizumab held for 6 weeks postoperatively then restarted.
surgeries: at age 55, patient underwent laparoscopic sigmoid colectomy with high ligation of inferior mesenteric artery.
at age 55, staged hepatic metastasectomy performed 6 weeks after colectomy; segment ivb segmentectomy and segment vii wedge resection completed with intraoperative ultrasound guidance.
adverse_events: after cycle 1 of folfox, patient experienced grade 2 nausea and cold-induced dysesthesia in hands characteristic of acute oxaliplatin toxicity; managed with dose-adjusted anti-emetics and cold avoidance counseling.
at age 55, patient developed grade 2 peripheral sensory neuropathy after 10 total cycles of oxaliplatin; oxaliplatin discontinued for final 2 planned cycles, completed with 5-fu/leucovorin alone.
at age 55, patient experienced significant anxiety regarding cancer recurrence requiring adjustment of psychiatric medications and referral to psycho-oncology.

treatments: at age 65, patient was started on androgen deprivation therapy with leuprolide acetate 22.5 mg intramuscular injection every 3 months for biochemical recurrence.
at age 67, enzalutamide 160 mg orally once daily was initiated for mcrpc in addition to continued leuprolide; baseline psa was 8.6 ng/ml.
at age 68, patient was transitioned to olaparib 300 mg orally twice daily given the presence of pathogenic brca2 mutation, in addition to continued leuprolide.
at age 70, docetaxel 75 mg/m2 intravenously every 3 weeks with prednisone 5 mg twice daily was initiated as next-line therapy for progressive mcrpc.
surgeries: at age 62, patient underwent robot-assisted radical prostatectomy with bilateral pelvic lymph node dissection; pathologic stage pt3an0m0 with positive surgical margins at the apex.
at age 70, patient underwent right hip hemiarthroplasty for pathologic fracture of the femoral neck secondary to metastatic prostate cancer.
radiation: at age 63, patient received adjuvant intensity-modulated radiation therapy (imrt) to the prostate bed, total dose 66 gy in 33 fractions, due to positive surgical margins.
at age 70, palliative radiation therapy was delivered to the right proximal femur, 30 gy in 10 fractions, for pain control and local disease management following surgical fixation.
adverse_events: at age 67, patient developed grade 2 fatigue and grade 1 hypertension (bp 148/92 mmhg) attributed to enzalutamide; antihypertensive medication amlodipine 5 mg was added.
at age 68, patient experienced grade 2 anemia (hemoglobin 9.8 g/dl) and grade 1 nausea while on olaparib; erythropoietin-stimulating agent was initiated.
at age 70, patient sustained a pathologic fracture of the right femoral neck related to bone metastatic disease; orthopedic consultation obtained.
at age 70, after cycle 3 of docetaxel, patient developed grade 3 neutropenia (anc 800/ul) and grade 2 peripheral neuropathy; docetaxel dose was reduced to 60 mg/m2.

treatments: at age 67, patient initiated cycle 1 of r-chop with rasburicase for tumor lysis prophylaxis, aggressive iv hydration, and close monitoring in the inpatient setting
at age 67, patient received cycle 2 of r-chop with 25 percent dose reduction of cyclophosphamide and doxorubicin and mandatory pegfilgrastim
at age 67, patient received cycle 3 of r-chop at reduced doses
at age 67, patient completed cycles 4, 5, and 6 of r-chop with continued dose reductions and growth factor support
at age 68, patient initiated salvage r-ice chemotherapy: rituximab 375 mg/m2, ifosfamide 5000 mg/m2, carboplatin auc 5, etoposide 100 mg/m2 days 1 through 3
at age 68, patient received 2 additional cycles of r-ice salvage with dose adjustments
adverse_events: at age 67, during cycle 1, patient developed tumor lysis syndrome with potassium 6.2 meq/l, uric acid 14.1, phosphorus 7.8, and creatinine rising to 2.4, managed with rasburicase, iv fluids, and kayexalate in the icu for 48 hours
at age 67, on day 10 of cycle 1, patient developed grade 4 febrile neutropenia with anc of 80 and temperature 103.2f, treated with vancomycin and cefepime, blood cultures negative
at age 67, after cycle 4, echocardiogram showed ejection fraction 48 percent decreased from baseline 55 percent, doxorubicin held for cycles 5 and 6 which were administered as r-cvp
at age 68, after cycle 1 of r-ice, patient developed grade 3 thrombocytopenia with platelet nadir of 18,000 and grade 4 neutropenia requiring prolonged hospitalization

treatments: at age 64, cycles 1 and 2 of vrd completed; m-spike decreased from 2.8 to 1.4 g/dl; serum free lambda light chain decreased to 72 mg/l; tolerated well with grade 1 fatigue only.

at age 64, cycles 3 and 4 of vrd completed; post-cycle 4 spep showed m-spike 0.4 g/dl; serum free lambda light chain 25 mg/l with kappa/lambda ratio 0.64 normalizing; response classified as very good partial response.

at age 64, completed 2 additional cycles of vrd for total of 6; post-induction spep showed no m-spike; immunofixation showed trace igg lambda band; classified as very good partial response bordering on complete response.

at age 65, underwent stem cell mobilization with g-csf plus plerixafor; collected 6.5 million cd34-positive cells per kilogram; received high-dose melphalan 200 mg/m2 followed by autologous stem cell transplant.
at age 65, started lenalidomide maintenance at 10 mg days 1 through 21 of 28-day cycle; tolerated well.
adverse_events: at age 64, after cycle 4, patient developed grade 1 peripheral neuropathy in toes bilaterally; bortezomib continued at full dose with close monitoring.
at age 65, post-transplant complicated by grade 2 mucositis and grade 2 nausea; no febrile neutropenia; engraftment on day plus 12; discharged day plus 15 in good condition.

at age 68, four years post-transplant; developed second primary malignancy with biopsy-proven basal cell carcinoma on left forearm; excised with clear margins; attributed to possible lenalidomide-associated increased risk; lenalidomide maintenance continued after multidisciplinary discussion.

treatments: at age 47, the patient began induction with 7+3 regimen of cytarabine 200 mg/m2 continuous infusion for seven days and daunorubicin 60 mg/m2 on days one through three, with midostaurin 50 mg orally twice daily from days eight through twenty-one.

at age 47, consolidation cycle one with high-dose cytarabine 3 g/m2 every twelve hours on days one, three, and five plus midostaurin was initiated. given persistent mrd positivity and dnmt3a co-mutation as an adverse prognostic factor, allogeneic transplant evaluation was pursued simultaneously.

at age 47, after two cycles of consolidation with continued mrd positivity by flow cytometry, the patient proceeded to allogeneic stem cell transplant from a ten-of-ten hla-matched unrelated donor with myeloablative busulfan and fludarabine conditioning.
adverse_events: at age 47, the patient developed prolonged febrile neutropenia lasting 22 days during induction. ct imaging showed bilateral lower lobe consolidations and bronchoalveolar lavage cultures grew stenotrophomonas maltophilia, treated with trimethoprim-sulfamethoxazole.
at age 47, grade 2 mucositis and grade 2 diarrhea occurred during induction, managed with supportive care including magic mouthwash and loperamide.
at age 47, during consolidation the patient developed clostridioides difficile-associated diarrhea with 10 watery stools per day, treated with oral fidaxomicin.

at age 47, the patient developed grade 2 acute gvhd of the skin and liver with bilirubin rising to 4.2 mg/dl and diffuse maculopapular rash over 60% body surface area, requiring systemic prednisone 1 mg/kg.

treatments: at age 61, patient initiated first-line folfox (5-fu 400 mg/m2 bolus, 2400 mg/m2 over 46 hours, leucovorin 400 mg/m2, oxaliplatin 85 mg/m2) plus bevacizumab 5 mg/kg every 2 weeks.
at age 62, postoperative adjuvant folfox (without bevacizumab) was administered for 4 additional cycles to complete a perioperative total of 12 cycles.
at age 63, patient started second-line folfiri (irinotecan 180 mg/m2, 5-fu, leucovorin) plus bevacizumab for recurrent hepatic metastases.
surgeries: at age 62, patient underwent laparoscopic sigmoid colectomy with en bloc mesenteric lymph node dissection and simultaneous right hepatectomy with wedge resection of segment ii lesion.
adverse_events: after cycle 4 of folfox, patient developed grade 2 peripheral sensory neuropathy in bilateral hands and feet, managed with dose reduction of oxaliplatin to 65 mg/m2.
at age 61, patient experienced grade 2 hypertension attributed to bevacizumab, controlled with amlodipine 5 mg daily.
at age 62, patient developed grade 3 peripheral neuropathy after cumulative oxaliplatin exposure, requiring discontinuation of oxaliplatin; switched to 5-fu/leucovorin maintenance.
at age 63, patient developed grade 2 diarrhea on folfiri, managed with loperamide and dose reduction of irinotecan to 150 mg/m2.

treatments: at age 72, patient started first-line folfiri (with irinotecan dose-adjusted for renal function) plus bevacizumab every 2 weeks.
at age 73, transitioned to second-line tas-102 (trifluridine/tipiracil) given concerns about regorafenib tolerability in the setting of ckd and hypertension.
surgeries: at age 72, patient underwent emergent right hemicolectomy with primary anastomosis for obstructing cecal tumor.
adverse_events: after cycle 3 of folfiri-bevacizumab, patient developed grade 2 diarrhea and grade 1 nausea; irinotecan reduced by 20% with improvement.

at age 72, patient developed grade 3 hypertension (bp 184/102) attributed to bevacizumab in the setting of pre-existing hypertension and ckd; required addition of amlodipine to existing antihypertensive regimen.
at age 73, patient experienced grade 2 fatigue limiting activities of daily living; discussed with patient and family, decided to continue treatment with supportive measures.
at age 73, developed grade 3 neutropenia on tas-102 requiring dose reduction and g-csf support with pegfilgrastim.

treatments: at age 34, combination ipilimumab 3 mg/kg plus nivolumab 1 mg/kg iv every 3 weeks was initiated for 4 cycles for braf v600e melanoma with brain metastasis, with immunotherapy chosen first per checkmate 204 data.
at age 34, after completing 4 induction cycles of ipilimumab/nivolumab, nivolumab 480 mg iv every 4 weeks was continued as maintenance.
surgeries: at age 34, wide local excision with 1-2 cm margins of the right neck melanoma was performed with primary closure.
at age 34, sentinel lymph node biopsy of the right cervical chain showed 1 of 2 sentinel nodes positive for a 1.2 mm metastatic melanoma deposit.
radiation: at age 34, stereotactic radiosurgery was delivered to the left occipital brain metastasis at 22 gy in 1 fraction with frameless technique.
adverse_events: at age 34, patient developed grade 2 immune-related colitis with watery diarrhea (6 stools/day) after cycle 3, managed with oral prednisone 1 mg/kg and 1 cycle delay of therapy.
at age 34, patient developed grade 1 immune-related thyroiditis with transient thyrotoxicosis (tsh

treatments: at age 52, patient initiated cycle 1 of r-chop at full standard doses with pegfilgrastim support
at age 52, patient received cycle 2 of r-chop without complications
at age 52, patient received cycle 3 of r-chop on schedule
at age 52, patient received cycles 4, 5, and 6 of r-chop, tolerating well with cumulative grade 1 neuropathy
radiation: at age 52, patient received consolidative involved-site radiation therapy to the mediastinum, 36 gy in 18 fractions, completed without significant toxicity
adverse_events: at age 52, after cycle 1, patient developed grade 1 nausea and grade 1 fatigue, managed conservatively
at age 53, patient reported persistent grade 1 peripheral neuropathy at 12-month follow-up, managed with gabapentin

treatments: at age 42, once the white blood cell count was controlled, induction chemotherapy was started with 7+3 consisting of cytarabine 200 mg/m2 continuous infusion for seven days and daunorubicin 60 mg/m2 on days one through three, with midostaurin 50 mg orally twice daily on days eight through twenty-one.

at age 42, the patient received consolidation with four cycles of high-dose cytarabine 3 g/m2 every twelve hours on days one, three, and five, with midostaurin 50 mg twice daily on days eight through twenty-one of each cycle.
at age 42, following completion of four consolidation cycles, the patient was started on midostaurin maintenance 50 mg orally twice daily, planned for twelve cycles.
adverse_events: at age 42, on day two of induction the patient developed clinical tumor lysis syndrome with potassium 6.8 meq/l, phosphorus 8.9 mg/dl, uric acid 13.6 mg/dl, and acute kidney injury with creatinine rising to 3.2 mg/dl, requiring transfer to the intensive care unit and initiation of continuous renal replacement therapy for 48 hours.

at age 42, the patient developed neutropenic fever on day ten with temperature 40.1 degrees celsius. chest ct showed bilateral ground-glass opacities and bronchoalveolar lavage was positive for pneumocystis jirovecii, treated with trimethoprim-sulfamethoxazole.
at age 42, prolonged pancytopenia during induction resulted in grade 3 mucositis and clostridium difficile colitis requiring oral vancomycin therapy.
at age 42, during consolidation cycle three the patient developed grade 2 maculopapular rash attributed to cytarabine, managed with topical corticosteroids and antihistamines.

treatments: at age 45, adjuvant pembrolizumab 200 mg iv every 3 weeks was initiated for resected stage iiib melanoma per keynote-054 data.
at age 46, pembrolizumab was resumed after hepatitis resolved to grade 0 and corticosteroids were tapered completely.
surgeries: at age 45, wide local excision with 2 cm margins was performed on the left calf with split-thickness skin graft closure.
at age 45, sentinel lymph node biopsy of the left inguinal region was performed, with 1 of 3 sentinel nodes positive for a micrometastatic melanoma deposit measuring 0.8 mm.
adverse_events: at age 46, patient developed grade 1 hypothyroidism (tsh 12.4 miu/l) likely immune-related, managed with levothyroxine supplementation without interruption of pembrolizumab.

at age 46, patient developed grade 2 immune-related hepatitis with alt 185 u/l and ast 156 u/l, pembrolizumab was held, and oral prednisone 1 mg/kg was initiated with transaminase normalization over 4 weeks.

treatments: at age 72, initiated carboplatin auc 6, paclitaxel 200 mg/m2, and pembrolizumab 200 mg iv every 3 weeks for unresectable stage iiib squamous nsclc.
at age 72, completed 4 cycles of carboplatin/paclitaxel/pembrolizumab. transitioned to pembrolizumab 200 mg iv every 3 weeks maintenance.
at age 73, resumed pembrolizumab 200 mg iv every 3 weeks with close pulmonary monitoring.
adverse_events: at age 72, after cycle 1, patient developed grade 2 nausea managed with ondansetron and dexamethasone. grade 1 fatigue reported.
at age 72, after cycle 2, patient reported grade 1 peripheral neuropathy in bilateral feet with tingling and numbness.

at age 73, after cycle 6 of pembrolizumab maintenance, patient developed grade 2 peripheral neuropathy with difficulty with fine motor tasks. paclitaxel previously discontinued; neuropathy persisting.

at age 73, after cycle 10 of pembrolizumab maintenance, patient developed new dry cough and dyspnea. ct chest showed ground-glass opacities in bilateral lower lobes suspicious for immune-related pneumonitis.
at age 73, grade 2 immune-related pneumonitis confirmed. symptoms improved after 2 weeks of corticosteroids. prednisone taper initiated.

treatments: at age 38, patient initiated cycle 1 of r-chop at full standard doses: rituximab 375 mg/m2, cyclophosphamide 750 mg/m2, doxorubicin 50 mg/m2, vincristine 1.4 mg/m2, prednisone 100 mg daily for 5 days
at age 38, patient received cycle 2 of r-chop without complications, pegfilgrastim administered on day 2
at age 38, patient received cycle 3 of r-chop on schedule without dose modifications
at age 38, patient received cycle 4 of r-chop without complications
at age 38, patient received cycle 5 of r-chop, continued to tolerate well
at age 38, patient completed cycle 6 of r-chop without significant toxicities
radiation: at age 38, given initial bulky stage ii disease, patient received consolidative involved-site radiation therapy to the right cervical and supraclavicular region, 30 gy in 15 fractions
adverse_events: at age 38, after cycle 1 of r-chop, patient experienced grade 2 nausea and grade 1 alopecia, managed with ondansetron and dexamethasone

treatments: at age 34, the patient was started on induction chemotherapy with the 7+3 regimen consisting of continuous infusion cytarabine 200 mg/m2 daily for seven days plus daunorubicin 60 mg/m2 on days one through three, combined with midostaurin 50 mg orally twice daily on days eight through twenty-one.

at age 34, consolidation cycle one was initiated with high-dose cytarabine 3 g/m2 every twelve hours on days one, three, and five, combined with midostaurin 50 mg orally twice daily on days eight through twenty-one.

at age 34, the patient proceeded to allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplant in first complete remission using a ten-of-ten hla-matched unrelated donor with myeloablative conditioning consisting of busulfan and cyclophosphamide.
adverse_events: at age 34, during induction the patient developed tumor lysis syndrome with uric acid rising to 12.4 mg/dl, potassium 6.1 meq/l, phosphorus 7.8 mg/dl, and calcium 6.9 mg/dl, requiring aggressive intravenous hydration and rasburicase administration.

at age 34, the patient developed neutropenic fever on day nine of induction with an absolute neutrophil count of 0 and temperature of 39.4 degrees celsius. blood cultures grew pseudomonas aeruginosa and empiric broad-spectrum antibiotics were initiated with cefepime and later escalated to meropenem.
at age 34, the patient experienced grade 3 oral mucositis requiring iv morphine patient-controlled analgesia and total parenteral nutrition for ten days during the aplastic phase of induction.

at age 34, during consolidation the patient developed invasive pulmonary aspergillosis confirmed by positive serum galactomannan antigen (index 3.2) and ct chest showing a cavitary nodule with halo sign. voriconazole was initiated with dose adjustment for midostaurin interaction.

at age 34, the patient developed grade 2 acute graft-versus-host disease involving the skin (stage 2 maculopapular rash over 40% body surface area) and upper gastrointestinal tract (persistent nausea and anorexia), treated with systemic corticosteroids.

treatments: at age 70, initiated carboplatin auc 5, pemetrexed 500 mg/m2, and pembrolizumab 200 mg iv every 3 weeks.
at age 70, completed 4 cycles of induction. transitioned to pemetrexed 500 mg/m2 plus pembrolizumab 200 mg iv every 3 weeks maintenance.
surgeries: at age 70, underwent indwelling pleural catheter (pleurx) placement for recurrent symptomatic left malignant pleural effusion.
adverse_events: at age 70, after cycle 2, patient developed grade 2 fatigue and grade 1 nausea. grade 1 elevated creatinine noted (1.3 mg/dl, baseline 0.9).
at age 70, after cycle 3, patient developed grade 2 nausea requiring additional antiemetics. grade 1 thrombocytopenia (platelets 112k).
at age 71, during maintenance cycle 4, patient developed grade 1 rash on trunk and extremities. managed with topical corticosteroids.
at age 71, after maintenance cycle 10, patient developed grade 1 hypothyroidism with tsh 7.9 miu/l. started levothyroxine 50 mcg daily.

treatments: at age 38, adjuvant dabrafenib 150 mg twice daily plus trametinib 2 mg daily was initiated for resected stage iiia braf v600e melanoma per combi-ad trial data.
surgeries: at age 38, wide local excision with 1-2 cm margins of the right forearm melanoma was performed with primary closure.
at age 38, sentinel lymph node biopsy of the right axilla identified 1 of 2 sentinel nodes positive for micrometastatic melanoma deposit measuring 0.4 mm detected by immunohistochemistry only.
adverse_events: at age 38, patient developed grade 2 pyrexia (temperature 38.9 c) and grade 1 chills within the first 2 weeks of dabrafenib/trametinib, managed with dabrafenib dose reduction to 100 mg twice daily and scheduled acetaminophen.
at age 38, patient developed grade 1 nausea and grade 1 rash attributed to trametinib, managed with ondansetron and topical corticosteroids.

treatments: at age 61, adjuvant nivolumab 480 mg iv every 4 weeks was started for resected stage iiic melanoma.
at age 63, combination dabrafenib 150 mg twice daily plus trametinib 2 mg daily was initiated for braf v600e-mutant metastatic melanoma given prior progression on adjuvant nivolumab.
surgeries: at age 61, wide local excision with 2 cm margins of the left shoulder melanoma site was performed with primary closure and all margins were negative.
at age 61, sentinel lymph node biopsy of the left axilla revealed 2 of 3 sentinel nodes positive for metastatic melanoma, largest deposit 5.1 mm with extracapsular extension present.
at age 61, completion left axillary lymph node dissection was performed yielding 22 lymph nodes, of which 1 additional node was positive for metastatic melanoma (total 3 positive nodes).
adverse_events: at age 61, patient developed grade 2 immune-related rash (maculopapular, covering 20% body surface area) managed with topical high-potency corticosteroids and oral antihistamines without nivolumab interruption.

at age 62, patient developed grade 2 immune-related hypophysitis with fatigue, headache, and low morning cortisol (2.1 mcg/dl), managed with hydrocortisone replacement and levothyroxine; nivolumab continued.
at age 63, patient developed grade 2 pyrexia (temperature 39.3 c) on dabrafenib/trametinib requiring brief dose interruption, successfully rechallenged at full dose with prophylactic acetaminophen.

treatments: at age 70, patient started first-line folfiri (irinotecan 180 mg/m2, 5-fu 400 mg/m2 bolus and 2400 mg/m2 over 46 hours, leucovorin 400 mg/m2) plus bevacizumab 5 mg/kg every 2 weeks.
at age 71, patient transitioned to second-line regorafenib 160 mg daily on a 3-weeks-on/1-week-off schedule.
at age 71, patient started tas-102 (trifluridine/tipiracil) 35 mg/m2 twice daily on days 1-5 and 8-12 of each 28-day cycle as third-line therapy.
adverse_events: after cycle 2 of folfiri-bevacizumab, patient developed grade 3 diarrhea requiring hospitalization for iv hydration and electrolyte repletion; irinotecan reduced to 150 mg/m2.
at age 70, patient experienced grade 2 hypertension (bp 162/98) attributed to bevacizumab, managed with lisinopril 20 mg daily.
at age 71, patient developed grade 2 alopecia on folfiri which was distressing but did not require dose modification.
at age 71, patient developed grade 3 hand-foot skin reaction on regorafenib, requiring dose reduction to 120 mg daily and supportive care with urea cream.
at age 71, patient experienced grade 2 fatigue and grade 1 elevation of liver transaminases (ast 72, alt 68) on regorafenib.

treatments: at age 61, initiated concurrent chemoradiation with carboplatin auc 2 weekly and pemetrexed 500 mg/m2 every 3 weeks during radiation.
at age 61, initiated durvalumab 10 mg/kg iv every 2 weeks as consolidation immunotherapy following concurrent chemoradiation, to be given for up to 12 months.
at age 62, resumed durvalumab after pneumonitis resolution. completed full 12 months of durvalumab consolidation.
radiation: at age 61, received definitive thoracic radiation therapy to left upper lobe primary and mediastinal lymph nodes, 60 gy in 30 fractions over 6 weeks.
adverse_events: at age 61, during concurrent chemoradiation week 4, patient developed grade 2 esophagitis with dysphagia to solids. managed with liquid diet and viscous lidocaine.
at age 61, at completion of chemoradiation, grade 2 fatigue, grade 2 esophagitis (improving), and grade 1 radiation dermatitis overlying left chest.

at age 62, after 4 months of durvalumab, patient developed grade 1 cough with mild dyspnea. ct showed bilateral ground-glass opacities not present on prior imaging. suspected grade 1 to 2 radiation recall pneumonitis versus immune-related pneumonitis.

treatments: at age 54, began folfirinox (5-fluorouracil 2400 mg/m2, leucovorin 400 mg/m2, irinotecan 180 mg/m2, oxaliplatin 85 mg/m2) every 14 days; cycle 1 of planned 12 cycles; full-dose regimen given young age and excellent performance status.
at age 54, continued folfirinox for total of 12 cycles; tolerated well with grade 1 neuropathy and intermittent grade 1 diarrhea.
at age 55, transitioned to second-line gemcitabine 1000 mg/m2 with nab-paclitaxel 125 mg/m2 on days 1, 8, 15 of 28-day cycles after progression on folfirinox-based approach.
at age 55, initiated third-line therapy with liposomal irinotecan 70 mg/m2 plus 5-fluorouracil 2400 mg/m2 and leucovorin 400 mg/m2 every 14 days.
surgeries: at age 55, patient underwent exploratory laparotomy with intention for pancreaticoduodenectomy; intraoperative findings revealed persistent encasement of the sma with tumor adherent to the artery that could not be safely separated; whipple procedure aborted; intraoperative radiation therapy (iort) with 15 gy delivered to the tumor bed; biopsy of peripancreatic tissue obtained.
radiation: at age 55, prior to surgical exploration, patient received neoadjuvant sbrt 25 gy in 5 fractions to the pancreatic head mass with fiducial marker-guided image guidance.
adverse_events: at age 54, grade 2 biliary obstruction with jaundice and pruritus; resolved after metal biliary stent placement; total bilirubin normalized to 1.4 mg/dl within two weeks.
at age 54, after cycle 3 of folfirinox, patient developed grade 2 nausea and grade 1 mucositis; antiemetic regimen optimized with addition of olanzapine.
at age 55, after cycle 2 of gemcitabine/nab-paclitaxel, developed grade 2 fatigue and grade 1 peripheral neuropathy; tolerated with supportive care.
